Chișinău apartment report
Listings (left) + Leaflet map (right). Click a listing to zoom/highlight the marker; click a marker to jump to the listing.
What you’re seeing: apartments for purchase (source-specific). Sector borders are approximate OSM admin relations. Late-evening busyness is a composite proxy within ~300m: food/drink density + transit density + culture venues + plaza/pedestrian-zone signal + pedestrian-friendly street length.
(filters)
Overlays (100m grid): each square shows OSM POI counts binned into a 100m×100m grid over Chișinău; color intensity is log-scaled density within that grid cell.
Map legend: pin confirmed pin unconfirmed pin mismatch
#1
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€169,950 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, equipped; autonomous heating + A/C).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/spec is compelling (2 baths + warm floors, autonomous heating, big enclosed balconies), but you should pressure-test the building quality/management and the price-per-sqm for this pocket of Ciocana.

Negotiation strategy

Use inspection leverage: ask for proof/age of the autonomous heating system, balcony heating legality/installation quality, and recent building maintenance (elevators, common areas). If anything is dated/DIY, negotiate. Target price range (guess, comps-dependent): ~€160k–€167k; if everything is genuinely high-quality and turnkey, expect less room.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, practical family apartment with rare comfort features (2 bathrooms, warm floors, autonomous heating) and strong everyday infrastructure. You are NOT buying: a central, nightlife-first micro-location; your upside is livability, not “old-town charm.”

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

Listing Summary

  • URL: https://999.md/ro/101568096
  • Title: Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.
  • Price: EUR 169950
  • Size: 96.6 sqm (stated)
  • Rooms: 3
  • Floor: 6/11 (stated in the ad)
  • Building type: Not stated in the ad.
  • Condition: Repaired/renovated (as stated)
  • Key amenities: autonomous heating; air conditioning; 2 bathrooms; 2 enclosed/heated balconies; furnished; appliances; underfloor heating (bathrooms)
  • Location: Ciocana; str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2

Apartment Description

Facts stated in the ad: The ad describes a 3-room apartment in Chișinău (Ciocana) at str. Mircea cel Bătrân 13/2, with a stated total area of 96.6 sqm. It mentions autonomous heating, air conditioning, that the apartment is repaired and furnished, equipped with appliances, and states 2 bathrooms.

Critical Evaluation of the Apartment

Facts stated in the ad:

  • Layout: 3 rooms; stated as decomandate (separate).
  • Size: 96.6 sqm stated.
  • Bathrooms: 2 stated (with underfloor heating).
  • Comfort: autonomous heating; air conditioning; enclosed/heated balconies (as stated).
  • Condition: repaired/renovated and furnished stated.

Reasonable inferences (verify):

  • Heated/enclosed balconies can add usable space, but verify insulation quality and whether modifications were permitted.
  • Noise exposure likely depends heavily on street vs courtyard orientation; verify on-site (day + evening).

Open questions / risks:

  • Not stated in the ad: monthly maintenance costs, utility bills, elevator condition, building year, legal/doc status, exact inclusions of furniture/appliances.
  • Verify: building management quality, upcoming repairs, and parking terms (if any).

Normalized scoring (inference; rubric v1)

  • Apartment score (0-10): 7.6 (strong size + comfort features; unknown building/elevator/fees)
  • Micro-area score (0-10): 7.4 (claimed amenities + strong OSM density nearby; verify noise)
  • Macro-area score (0-10): 6.9 (Ciocana is practical; lifestyle/prestige depends on micro-location)

Scoring note: scores are a standardized estimate for ranking; they are not facts from the ad and should be validated at viewing / via documents.

Pros

  • Large stated size (96.6 sqm) for a 3-room unit.
  • Autonomous heating + A/C stated.
  • Two bathrooms stated.
  • Two enclosed/heated balconies stated.
  • Repaired and furnished, appliances included (as stated).

Cons

  • Building-level details and running costs are not stated.
  • Noise/orientation details are not stated.

Risks / Unknowns

  • Monthly costs / utilities unknown.
  • Elevator/building maintenance unknown.
  • Exact inclusions (furniture/appliances) not fully specified.
  • Balcony enclosure/heating setup should be verified.

Verdict

  • Resident verdict: Promising on paper as a family-sized apartment, but verify building upkeep, noise, and running costs.
  • Investor verdict: Potentially rentable if the building/location check out; confirm costs and demand vs price.
  • Best for: families wanting space + comfort features; buyers comfortable verifying at viewing.
  • Price positioning: Not confirmable from the ad alone; implied ~EUR 1759/sqm from stated size—comps check is essential.

Micro-area (local guide)

Facts stated in the ad: The ad claims strong nearby infrastructure within ~5 minutes’ walk (kindergarten, school, market, supermarkets including Kaufland, bank) and good public transport access.

Reasonable inferences (verify): If accurate, daily errands should be very convenient; expect typical traffic/noise patterns for a major street.

Risks / questions: Verify actual walk times and do an evening noise check at the building entrance and inside the unit.

Macro-area (local guide)

Facts stated in the ad: Sector/district is Ciocana.

Reasonable inferences (resident viewpoint): Sector labels help, but block-by-block reality matters; prioritize noise, building condition, and commute convenience once the exact micro-location is confirmed.

  • Price: €169,950
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica (Grădina Botanică) · ул. Грэдина Ботаникэ, 18/1
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build shell/“white variant”: structurally new, but you’re buying a project (finishes + kitchen + lighting + AC + wardrobes).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive size (105 m²) in a green micro-area, but the listing has a major consistency red flag (floor vs number of floors) and it’s unfinished.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165k–€175k (best-effort guess; comps unknown). Push hard on (1) completion cost + time, (2) documentation clarity (new-build commissioning/ownership papers), and (3) the floor/building-height mismatch. If the “16th floor” is real, demand proof of actual building storeys, elevator spec, and wind/noise exposure; if it’s not real, use it as credibility leverage.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, sunny, new-build 3-room shell with modern building systems (elevator/video/intercom) and park access. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home, nor a proven building with known maintenance/condo-fee history. Big deal can go wrong if: (1) the building’s legal status/commissioning is incomplete, delaying registration/mortgage/resale; (2) panoramic glazing + high-floor exposure creates overheating, drafts, or persistent wind noise without proper shading/AC plan.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Богатая инфраструктура данного района города чистый воздух, зелёный район, школы, садики, парк, ресторан, магазины, банки, нотариусы и многое другое...”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, “euro renovation”, fully furnished + full appliance stack; likely only minor personalization.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great turnkey package in a central new-build, but at this price you should push hard on proof of quality (developer/building management) and total monthly carrying costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €199 000–€210 000. Use “risk discount” leverage: high floor + big windows (heat/glare), new-build finishing quality uncertainty, and agency listing. Ask for documentation (handover/acceptance, utilities, heating service history) and negotiate on friction. Target price range (guess): 205k–215k if everything checks out; if any quality/maintenance red flags appear, treat 219k as a walk-away anchor.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, central, turnkey 3-room + living with strong comfort features (autonomous heat, AC, elevator, security systems).
You are NOT buying: a “quiet, low-risk” building by default—new-builds can hide management/maintenance issues; verify the building ecosystem, not just the apartment.
High floor + panoramic glass can feel premium… or punish you with summer heat and wind noise if execution is mediocre.

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work (white-box/“variantă albă” means you’re budgeting time + cash before living there).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive new-build top floor, but you’re paying a premium while taking developer + top-floor risk and unknowns (layout, terrace, build quality).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €185k–€200k (comps unknown, so this is a best-effort anchor). Push discounts/credits tied to verifiable uncertainties: (1) top-floor waterproofing/terrace details + written warranty scope, (2) elevator spec + maintenance plan + HOA budget, (3) confirmed net vs gross area and what’s included (terrace/balcony), (4) utility connections and final acceptance paperwork. If any of these are fuzzy, treat it as money-off.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, top-floor 3-room unit in Sculeni with strong “city convenience” signals (lots of parks nearby, some groceries/food, transit). You are NOT buying: a turnkey home or guaranteed quiet—Calea Ieșilor is a major corridor and 13/13 amplifies wind/heat/waterproofing sensitivity. Two ways this deal goes wrong: (1) terrace/roof detailing leaks or causes ongoing maintenance fights; (2) promised “penthouse” feel is undercut by noisy exposure (traffic/arterial) or weak building management costs that show up after handover.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances included; modern building services).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — looks like a polished, comfort-heavy apartment in a lively micro-area, but you should push on price and verify noise/parking/elevator realities before paying “turnkey premium.”

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €205 000–€216 000. Treat the listing as “priced for convenience.” Your leverage is uncertainty: actual building quality, ongoing fees, parking situation, and boulevard noise. Target price range (guess): 5–10% below ask if the boulevard-facing exposure/parking/HOA costs aren’t clearly favorable; otherwise negotiate on inclusions (furniture, appliances, parking) more than headline price.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, fully-equipped, comfort-first home (autonomous heat + underfloor heating + AC + security/closed yard). You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-activity pocket—this is an amenity-dense zone that can also mean traffic, crowds, and parking friction. If you hate uncertainty, demand: exact orientation (courtyard vs boulevard), proof of sound insulation performance, and a clear parking arrangement.

€140,000 · 4 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready, but “cosmetic repair” + panel-building realities mean budget for upgrades after purchase.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (4 rooms, 87.1 sqm on paper, triple exposure, 3 loggias) but the “de facto >100 sqm” marketing and panel-building risks should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (best-effort; comps unknown). Levers to press: (1) insist the valuation/mortgage uses the documented 87.1 sqm only; treat loggias as a bonus, not paid area, (2) ask for documented recent building works (roof/entrance/elevators/heating risers) — if missing/dated, price that future capex in, (3) noise/orientation check: verify which sides face the boulevard vs courtyard; if bedrooms face traffic, discount.

Bottom line

You are buying a big, family-oriented Ciocana apartment with convenience (groceries, parks, schools) and lots of “extra” outdoor space via loggias. You are NOT buying guaranteed extra legal square meters — loggias inflate lived space, not the official area. If the quiet/sunlight checks pass, this can be a very livable long-term hold; if traffic + building condition disappoint, it becomes an overpriced panel compromise fast.

Nu are mirosuri. Geamuri termopane pe tot perimetrul. 3 loje, una fiind cea m…”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 11 grocery shops, 14 food/drink places, 36 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~49.5/km², grocery density ~38.9/km², transit-stop density ~21.2/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Rooms: 4
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana

€91,800 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Nicolae Dimo 98
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build, delivered as “white variant” and still under construction with a far handover (Dec 2027).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive price per size for a new complex, but you’re buying a promise (timeline/quality) more than a finished apartment.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €85,000–€90,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have true local comps for this exact tranche). Levers: (1) delivery date risk (Dec 2027) + payment schedule; ask for a written milestone plan and penalties/discounts if delayed, (2) clarify what “white variant” includes (heating boiler/radiators, electrical panel, plaster level, doors) and price any missing items into a discount, (3) confirm the exact unit orientation/view and noise exposure before committing.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, amenity-heavy complex lifestyle (elevator, video surveillance, secured courtyard, parking options) with autonomous heating. You are NOT buying a finished, immediately usable home — budget time and money for the fit-out, and accept schedule uncertainty. If the developer execution slips or specs get “value engineered,” your upside compresses fast.

#8
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€390,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Tudor Strișcă, 8/7
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (high-end finish), but "lux" claims should be stress-tested against documents and building systems.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the product sounds premium (163 m², top floor-ish, private lift, big heating spec), but at €390k you’re paying for *uniqueness*; you need proof the “private lift + terrace/BBQ exit + garage” are cleanly legal/registered.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €340k–€370k (best-effort; comps unknown, so this is a risk-adjusted offer band rather than a market-derived comp). Ask for (1) cadastral extract showing 163 m² and exact layout, (2) explicit title/registration for the double garage + any terrace/private outdoor area, (3) written confirmation of the private lift access arrangement (HOA/building admin) and ongoing costs. Negotiation lever: if any of those items are “usage rights” rather than owned/registered area (or if there are restrictions/extra monthly fees for lift/security/parking), price must move down materially.

Bottom line

You are buying: a trophy-style, privacy-forward apartment with real engineering signals (water underfloor heating, panoramic glazing, smart-home) in a park-adjacent Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: a universally liquid, easy-to-sell unit — the value is tied to specific features (private lift/garage/terrace) and the building’s governance. Two ways this deal goes wrong: (1) the garage/terrace/private access turns out partially unregistered or limited by HOA rules, harming resale and financing; (2) high-end systems (underfloor heating, smart-home integration, panoramic windows) have expensive maintenance/repair pathways and unclear installer warranties.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Se vinde apartment LUX în Botanica: 163 m², 2 dormitoare + living + bucătărie separată, 2 băi, balcon/logie.”.

The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden), Parking / garage availability and terms. Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s listed as “no renovation”, so budget time/money before you can enjoy it.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great functional layout in a central, amenity-dense micro-area, but “no renovation” is your leverage and your risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €78,000–€83,000 (comps not provided; this is a leverage-based range). Levers: (1) renovation scope uncertainty (electrics/plumbing, windows, bathrooms x2), (2) elevator/building condition, (3) parking is “open” (not guaranteed). Concrete check to tie to price: ask for building maintenance status + recent repairs plan (roof/elevator/riser pipes) and confirm heating billing/comfort for centralized heat.

Bottom line

You are buying: a very usable 3-room / 2-bath layout in Centru with elevator and strong everyday walkability. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort — expect dust, delays, and real renovation decisions (especially with two bathrooms and loggias). Two ways this deal can go wrong: renovation costs blow out (hidden wiring/plumbing issues) and noise/traffic exposure disappoints depending on orientation and courtyard vs street-facing rooms.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — renovated, furnished, and fully equipped, but the “developer selling” angle still warrants paperwork diligence.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong apartment spec (duplex, 130 m², 2 baths, floor heating), but location vibes look more residential than lively and the price is premium for Botanica unless the building is truly top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €190,000–€205,000 (I don’t have local comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) “open” parking vs guaranteed space, (2) elevator/roof/upper-floor maintenance risk, (3) developer sale terms and any finishing/technical warranties, (4) noise/wind and summer heat load at floor 10.

Bottom line

You are buying a big, modern-feeling duplex apartment that lives more like a small house in the sky. You are NOT buying a restaurant-heavy, late-evening pedestrian neighborhood — it reads greener/quiet with essentials nearby. If the building is well-managed and the finishes are real (not photo-magic), it’s a compelling lifestyle buy at the right price.

€210,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. 31 August 1989, 59
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, with autonomous heating + modern comforts — but verify the renovation quality and what exactly is included.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — this is a good Centru package (quiet courtyard + autonomous heating + parking) but price needs justification via docs, building condition, and true ownership/parking rights.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €190,000–€205,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Levers to push: - Parking: confirm it’s deeded/contracted/assigned and transferable; if it’s “informal,” discount hard. - Heating/floor heating: request proof of permits/installation + recent winter bills; if underspecified, use as a concession lever. - Building/stairwell/roof: 4-floor stock can hide roof/waterproofing issues; ask for last repairs + maintenance costs; negotiate if common areas look tired. - “Smart home/panoramic windows”: ask for brand list + warranties; negotiate if it’s marketing fluff.

Bottom line

You are buying: a quiet, courtyard-facing, renovated 93 m² home in the most liquid part of Chișinău with autonomous heating and day-to-day walkability. You are NOT buying: guaranteed low-noise (street can be busy nearby) or elevator convenience; resale/rent appeal depends on stair comfort and building condition. Two ways this deal can go wrong: parking turns out non-transferable/just a courtyard habit; or “quality renovation” hides shortcuts (moisture, uneven floors, noisy plumbing) that only show after a season.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (the listing claims a good renovation + autonomous heating), but verify building/legal status because it reads like a small ground-household setup.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the core proposition is strong (Centru + yard + 2 garages), but uncertainty around type of building/ownership structure is the make-or-break.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €205k–€220k (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted offer to price in legal/technical uncertainty and renovation variance). Push on: (1) exact property type (“apartment” vs part of a house / land share), (2) cadastral extract + encumbrances, (3) autonomous heating permits/installation papers, (4) what exactly transfers with the deal (both garages? land/courtyard rights?), (5) moisture/insulation and soundproofing in an older “fond locativ” context. Use the seller’s own “possible bargaining” note to anchor.

Bottom line

You are buying: central walkability with an unusually private feel (yard + parking + garages). You are NOT buying: a standard, easy-to-resell elevator-building apartment unless the documents and building type confirm it. If paperwork is clean, it’s a scarce product; if it’s messy, walk away fast.

Key line from the ad: “Центр города на земле большой двор на 3 семьи два гаража, металлический и котельцовый, 3 минуты от улицы Виеру комнаты раздельные, санузел совмещённый хорлший ремонт, автономка, по цене возможен торг”.

Additional stated detail: “Caracteristici (999.md panels): Balcon/ lojie; Compartimentare; Dezvoltator; Etaj; Euroreparație; Fond locativ; Grup sanitar; Inălțimea tavanelor; Living; Loc de parcare; Număr de camere; Număr de etaje; Starea aparta…”. Also noted: “Центр города на земле большой двор на 3 семьи два гаража, металлический и котельцовый, 3 минуты от улицы Виеру комнаты раздельные, санузел совмещённый хорлший ремонт, автономка, по цене возможен торг”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 4. (Structured field) Price: €230 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Balcon/ lojie, Compartimentare, Dezvoltator, Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Grup sanitar, Inălțimea tavanelor, Living, Loc de parcare, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață bucătărie, Suprafață locativă, Suprafață totală, Tip clădire. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): Alexandru Botezatu.

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, equipped; low risk if the “quality repair” claim checks out in person.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (100 m², 3 beds + living, new brick building, floor 2/8, autonomous heat), but you’re paying a premium and need proof on build/finish quality and legal cleanliness.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €220,000–€235,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have reliable comps here). Anchor with: (1) verify actual net area vs total, (2) elevator/parking terms, (3) noise/privacy at floor 2, (4) HVAC/boiler service history, (5) “direct from owner” vs developer representation. If parking isn’t included or documents are messy, push harder.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, family-friendly, modern 3-bedroom layout with two baths in a secured newer complex in Centru. You are NOT buying: a quiet top-floor lightbox or a bargain; floor-2 urban noise/visibility and premium pricing are the trade-offs. If the finishes and paperwork pass inspection, this is a strong “pay for convenience” home — just don’t overpay by default.

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Not move-in ready: new-build in progress, sold as “white variant” (expect finishing costs + timeline risk).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional 3-room layout in a new block with parking options, but you’re paying new-build pricing while taking construction/hand-over and fit-out risk.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a written delivery timeline, penalties/escrow terms, and a full spec sheet (windows, heating, elevator brand, sound insulation). Push for incentives instead of headline discount: included underground parking, discounted finishing package, or staged payments tied to milestones. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €112k–€118k if no parking included / €118k–€125k if underground parking + clear handover terms are included.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, family-friendly new-build shell with strong day-to-day convenience (groceries/transit) and a sensible 2nd-floor plan. You are NOT buying: guaranteed near-term move-in, a fully finished home, or a vibrant central nightlife micro-location. You’re also buying developer execution risk—make the contract do the heavy lifting.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€139,900 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Petru Zadnipru
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, equipped; expect only cosmetic tweaks after inspection.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and size for the money, but panel/MS-series + renovation quality uncertainty means you should buy only at the right price.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €128,000–€135,000 (comps not provided; range is a best-effort risk-adjusted bid). Levers: (1) Ask for proof/clarity on heating setup (centralized billing, recent costs, any building-level issues) and use any ambiguity to anchor a discount; (2) treat “furnished + appliances” as convenience, not value—price it as optional and negotiate if items are dated; (3) request building maintenance/elevator/roof status (9-floor block risk) and push price down if the building fund/repairs look weak.

Bottom line

You are buying a big, practical family apartment in a transit-connected micro-area with parks and everyday amenities. You are NOT buying a boutique building or guaranteed quiet—panel blocks can amplify noise and maintenance surprises. This deal goes wrong if the “euro renovation” is cosmetic (hidden wiring/plumbing issues) or if the block has deferred repairs (elevator/roof/façade) that later hit you via costs and resale drag.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — decent entry price for a 3-room in Botanica, but you’re buying an older walk-up stock with limited on-site amenities and unknowns on building/condition.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €85,000–€90,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided). Use these levers: (1) 4th floor out of 5 (walk-up fatigue narrows buyer pool), (2) “cosmetic/locativ” condition usually hides kitchen/bath, wiring, and window/insulation spend, (3) centralized heating can mean comfort OR seasonal control/fee uncertainty—ask for recent bills and building heat performance.

Bottom line

You are buying: a functional 3-room, ~60 m², older-era apartment that works as a practical home immediately. You are NOT buying: a modern building experience (elevator/new systems) or a lively, amenity-dense street life. If the balcony/orientation and heating costs check out, it’s a solid “value not vibes” deal in Botanica.

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.

€94,300 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · str. Ialoveni, 100/2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

New-build developer unit: likely “white-box” or semi-finished — move-in depends on what finish package is actually included.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — decent “new build 3-room” proposition on paper, but you’re buying developer execution + surrounding convenience risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €86,000–€92,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-discount range). Use developer leverage points: ask for a direct discount OR value add (finished interior package, kitchen/wardrobes credit, parking/storage, notary/transaction costs, better payment schedule). Demand a written spec: exact finishes, insulation/windows, heating system, elevator brand, and delivery timeline with penalties.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern monolithic-structure apartment in a developer complex with “new build” appeal. You are NOT buying a proven building (yet) or a walk-out-the-door neighbourhood convenience cluster. If the delivery/spec is strong and the discount lands, it can be a solid functional home — otherwise it’s a price trap with finishing surprises.

Negotiation strategy

You are buying: a modern 3-room family layout in a controlled complex with autonomous heating, elevator, security systems, and underground parking option. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in or guaranteed 2026 delivery quality; and you’re not buying a high-street, restaurant-heavy micro-area (nearby amenities look more “daily needs” than “nightlife”).

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s a new-build developer unit with strong “spec sheet” promises; confirm actual stage/fit-out, handover timeline, and what’s included (finishes vs shell).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the building package sounds good (monolith structure, ventilated facade, gas boiler, premium lift), but you’re paying developer pricing without seeing the hard details (true layout, net area, view/noise, and delivery/acceptance conditions).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €135,000–€145,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: exact usable vs total area, balcony/loggia count, orientation/view, floorplan efficiency, parking/storage availability and price, contract milestones, penalties for delays, warranty terms, and what “premium” actually includes. If the unit is already finished/ready, your leverage shifts to comparing against similar new-build listings nearby.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build proposition in Botanica with a “comfort + energy efficiency” narrative (ventilated insulated facade, triple glazing, high ceilings, gas boiler). You are NOT buying: a proven resale with known neighbors/maintenance history — developer promises must be verified on-site and in contract. You are also buying: the micro-area’s calm/green bias (lots of nearby parks in OSM signals) more than a restaurant/nightlife hub (low food/drink count within 300m).

€189,700 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Cărtușa 103
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready (new-build “premium” pitch + AC + elevator + autonomous heating), but the penthouse/terrace details can hide expensive punch-list items.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great size (146 m²) and penthouse layout, but you’re buying the building’s maintenance reality (roof/terrace, elevator reliability, HOA quality) as much as the apartment.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170,000–€182,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps for this building/street, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Levers to press: - Terrace/roof: ask for written details on waterproofing history, warranties, and any prior leak/repair reports; use uncertainty here to justify a price cut or seller-paid remediation. - “Two closed garages” sold additionally: push to bundle at a discounted package price or get one included; if they won’t, reduce the apartment price accordingly. - 10/10 + elevator: request service logs / downtime history; any weak answers = price reduction for resale/liquidity risk.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, two-level, view-driven home with a big social zone (39 m² kitchen) and terrace-lifestyle. You are NOT buying: an easy-to-resell, low-maintenance unit—penthouse premiums cut both ways. Two ways this goes wrong: terrace waterproofing becomes a recurring cost; elevator/HOA quality turns “premium” into daily friction (noise, fees, inconsistent maintenance).

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — explicitly “needs renovation”, but the layout + 2 baths + 2 balconies gives you a solid canvas.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good size (≈72.6 sqm) and functional plan, but 9/10 + renovation + unknown building condition means price must compensate.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €85,000–€92,000 (best-effort; I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a renovation-risk discount framework). Ask for a clear, written list of what “needs renovation” means (electrical, plumbing, windows already done, etc.) and price each line. Negotiation lever (verifiable uncertainty): confirm heating reality and monthly costs for centralized heating (latest invoices + whether there are building-level issues); if costs are high or system is unreliable, that’s immediate leverage. Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) building-level problems (roof, elevator, risers) surface after you start renovating and you pay twice; (2) 9th-floor comfort issues (wind/noise/overheating, elevator downtime) make resale/tenant demand softer than you expect.

Bottom line

You are buying a big, practical family layout in Ciocana with day-to-day convenience (groceries/parks) and open parking. You are NOT buying turn-key — renovation budget + timeline discipline will make or break this. If the building/elevator/heating check out, this can be a smart “buy the ugly one” deal at the right price.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediată apropiere găsiți: bănci, saloane de frumusețe, supermarketuri, grădinițe, licee, etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Street (from listing fields): Tudor Strișcă.

Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, renovated, furnished, appliances included — but verify build quality and systems (heating/ventilation) at this floor.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “turnkey” package in Centru and likely a solid new-build, but the price looks premium for 75 m² and you’re buying the developer/building execution as much as the layout.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235k–€245k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Anchor on (1) any lack of parking/storage, (2) proof of autonomous heating specs + running costs, (3) documentation/hand-over details, and (4) any signs of cosmetic-first renovation.

Bottom line

You are buying: a high-floor, new-build, designer-finished 3-room with a modern open living/kitchen and 2 bathrooms in a practical Centru pocket. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet (high floor helps, but traffic/wind/stack noise can bite) or guaranteed top-tier construction just because it’s “new” and looks polished. You’re paying for convenience and finish — make sure the building fundamentals (management, utilities, noise, elevators) deserve the premium.

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional package at a plausible price, but 9/9 and “series block” quality risk should buy you a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €78,000–€83,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) top-floor/roof liability, (2) any elevator downtime, (3) building management fees/repairs, (4) layout mismatch (listing says “3 bedrooms” yet “3 rooms” headline), (5) documentation for “new construction” claim vs typical series block.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 71 m², 3-room Buiucani home with autonomous heating and decent day-to-day convenience. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/prime-central vibes, nor risk-free top-floor ownership unless roof + elevator + insulation check out. If the building is genuinely solid and the roof is recently redone, this can be a very clean value play at the right number.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready (new-build shell/turnkey depends on what “Smart Home” package includes; expect you’ll still budget for finishing/furnishings unless explicitly delivered finished).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid modern 3-room size in a new complex, but price needs to justify high-floor tower trade-offs and the relatively thin immediate amenity/park signal.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €95,000–€102,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: developer inventory/competition inside the same complex, finish level included/excluded, any delays in commissioning/management fees, and the high-floor risk (wind/noise/elevator dependence). If it’s delivered fully finished with documented smart-home spec + quality materials, your range can drift upward.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, high-floor, new-build 3-room with 2 bathrooms, balcony/terrace, elevator, autonomous heating, and “security-stack” features (intercom/video/alarm) in a developer-led complex. You are NOT buying: a proven, leafy, café-dense pedestrian micro-neighbourhood right outside the door (nearby food/parks signal looks weak). High floor = views/light + less street noise, but also more reliance on building maintenance and elevator uptime.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build “white variant” delivery means you’re buying a shell and the real cost/timeline starts after handover.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good layout on paper, but you’re taking completion/quality risk and paying today for a 2025 promise.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112k–€120k (best-effort; comps not provided). Anchor with (1) “2025 completion + developer delivery risk”, (2) your fit-out budget for a white variant, (3) clarify whether underground parking is included vs. extra. Concrete lever: make price contingent on written specs (heating type, floor heating zones, glazing/panoramic windows, elevator count) and a penalty/discount if handover slips.

Bottom line

You are buying: a 3-room + living, 89 m², mid-floor unit in a planned complex with autonomous heating, elevator, and on-site play/recreation. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment, nor a proven “already lived-in” building with known neighbor noise, maintenance fees, and management quality. Two ways this can go wrong: (1) handover delays or “spec drift” (materials/systems not matching showroom promises), (2) post-handover surprises (humidity/finishing defects, elevator/parking access rules, higher-than-expected monthly maintenance).

#31
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€83,900 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · str. Nicolae Dimo, 29/3
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — sold “as-is” with no renovation; budget for full interior + systems check.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid micro-location (second line by a park, low-ish floor), but the “no repair” condition and likely older building risks should translate into a meaningful discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €75,000–€80,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have direct comps here). Anchor with (1) full renovation scope, (2) any wiring/plumbing/heating unknowns, (3) balcony/thermal performance and window condition, and (4) documentation/plan consistency for the stated areas. Ask the seller to confirm the cadastral area vs advertised total and whether the balcony is included in any way.

Bottom line

You are buying: a 2nd-floor, 3-room, small-to-medium footprint apartment in a green, family-practical pocket. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort — expect time, noise, and cash outlay to make it livable. This deal goes wrong if: the 3-room layout is “railroad/tight” and renovation can’t fix functionality; or the building’s shared infrastructure (roof, risers, electrical) is tired and starts generating surprise costs after you move in.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — renovated (“euroreparație”), functional 3-bed layout, 2 bathrooms; verify build quality + utilities under load (top floor).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (Centru + 81 m² + top-floor views + underground parking), but at €180k you should force clarity on building quality, elevators, and real comps.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps not provided): €165k–€175k. Push on: “13/13 risk” (roof/overheating, elevator downtime), agency listing premium, and demand proof (recent comparable sales/rents). Make the underground parking and renovation quality the swing factors: if both are truly strong, pay closer to the top of the range.

Bottom line

You are buying: a central, amenity-dense micro-area with a big-city feel and a top-floor panoramic apartment that’s easy to live in day 1. You are NOT buying: a ‘risk-free’ building — top-floor + elevator dependence means you must diligence maintenance, roof, and service charges. If the building management is solid and the parking is deeded/real, this becomes a clean long-term hold; otherwise, don’t overpay for the view.

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong plan + modern complex, but top-floor exposure + 2026 completion + finishing costs mean price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €128k–€138k (best-effort). I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a risk-adjusted bid: push on 2026 delivery, “white box” finishing budget, and top-floor trade-offs; ask for incentives (parking spot discount, staged payments, or a finishing credit).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room in a modern complex with terrace, 2 bathrooms, autonomous heating, and strong building amenities/security. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in, certainty on handover timing, or a high-café/late-evening street-life micro-location. Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Not move-in ready: new-build “white box” and delivery planned for end-2028 — you’re buying a promise + a future renovation budget.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive €/sqm on paper for a large 3-room, but the real risk is timeline + developer execution + total “all-in” cost.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82k–€88k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Push for: (1) price cut or “developer promo” tied to early payment, (2) parking spot or storage included/discounted, (3) contract clarity on handover spec + penalties for delays. A concrete lever: ask for written specs on sound insulation / glazing / HVAC and link any ambiguity to a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a sizeable 3-room in a planned 12-floor complex with underground parking and security features — mainly a future home. You are NOT buying: a walk-outside-and-live neighborhood right now, nor a guaranteed 2028 timeline, nor a finished apartment budget. Two ways this goes wrong: handover delays beyond 2028 that trap your capital; or “white box” scope gaps (walls/utility points/soundproofing) that balloon finishing costs and reduce comfort.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: “white-box” finish in a new-build means the hard infrastructure is there (heating, glazing, elevator), but you’re still budgeting time + cash for full interior fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the spec is attractive (new-build, 92 m², 2 baths, underfloor heating), but Poșta Veche is micro-area sensitive and price only makes sense if the building/HOA, sound insulation, and exposure/views check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145,000–€152,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Use “white-box” cost-to-finish + any unknowns (exact building quality, elevator count/reliability, parking deed/rights, utilities/commissioning, HOA fees) as leverage. Ask for: (1) exact finish scope, (2) acceptance/commissioning docs, (3) parking terms (owned vs allocated), (4) orientation + noise sources, (5) total monthly building costs.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, higher-floor new-build shell with strong comfort features (autonomous + underfloor heating, big windows, security/video, elevator) and a family-friendly layout. You are NOT buying a turnkey home — finishing costs, timelines, and workmanship risk are on you. At this price, the building’s execution (soundproofing, common areas, management) matters more than the feature list.

The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Poșta Veche, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely close to move-in ready (new-build, autonomous heating, modern windows/door), but confirm actual finish level + terrace/loggia waterproofing on the top floors.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong space/plan for the money, but top-floor risk + micro-area amenity density is modest; you want price protection and clean documentation.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: full spec sheet (finish stage, materials), HOA/management terms, elevator/roof/terrace warranty, underground parking cost/availability, and final commissioning/ownership docs from the developer. Target price range: €130,000–€138,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided, so treat as a negotiation anchor rather than a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, top-floor 3-room with two bathrooms + real storage and underground parking in a new complex. You are NOT buying: a high-footfall, restaurant-heavy central lifestyle micro-area. If the terrace/roof/elevator/management checks out, this can be a very livable “space-first” buy — otherwise your future headaches will be maintenance, not layout.

#38
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Dumbrava, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Dumbrava, Chișinău mun.)
€159,900 · 3 rooms · Centru · strada Durlești, Dumbrava
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (furnished + appliances + autonomous heating + floor heating), but verify finish quality and humidity/noise given 1st floor.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good spec package (new build, elevator, autonomous + floor heating) but the 1st floor and “Dumbrava/near Durlești” positioning should be priced with a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for documents + building details first, then price it like a ground-floor unit: target range €145,000–€152,000 (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted anchor). If seller resists, negotiate for value: included furniture/appliances list + warranty/service records for heating/floor system + window/insulation confirmation. Concrete lever: the biggest uncertainty you can verify fast is ground-floor exposure (street-side vs courtyard) — use measured noise levels at rush hour + privacy/sightline issues as the justification for a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a newer-build 77 m², well-equipped family layout with strong “comfort systems” (autonomous + floor heating) in a greener, calmer pocket. You are NOT buying: a central, walk-out-to-restaurants vibe; also not a slam-dunk price unless the 1st-floor drawbacks are offset (courtyard orientation, good soundproofing). Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) ground-floor noise/privacy and weak natural light if it faces a street or tight spacing; (2) heating/floor-heating execution problems (uneven zones, leaks, high running costs) if installation/controls are cheap or undocumented.

#39
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€335,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · bd. Ștefan Cel Mare Și Sfânt 132
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (newer building + quality renovation + autonomous/underfloor heating signals “done” rather than “project”).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2 bedrooms + courtyard/playground + schools/kindergartens nearby) Investor: ➖ (ultra-central helps liquidity, but entry price per m² looks steep unless finishes/building are truly premium) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (right on the main boulevard; walk-to-everything; restaurants/transit density)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location + modern building package, but the price implies “prime” and you need to verify noise, build quality, and actual net area.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on (1) boulevard noise/air + (2) resale ceiling + (3) any ambiguity in the stated area (the text shows 72.2 m², but there’s also a suspicious “722 m²” artifact). Ask for: cadastral extract, exact usable vs total area, heating bills, HOA/maintenance, and what’s included (kitchen, built-ins). Target price range: €300,000–€320,000 (comps not provided, so treat as a negotiating band, not a valuation). If they won’t move: request included furniture/appliances, parking terms (if any), and a fast-close discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a central, high-convenience, newer-building 3-room with modern heating and a “closed courtyard” lifestyle. You are NOT buying: quiet, low-traffic serenity—being on the main boulevard almost always means noise/dust trade-offs. This works best if you value daily walkability and want zero renovation drama more than maximum space-per-euro.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished + appliances, autonomous heating; 8/9 with elevator reduces daily friction.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground nearby; school/kindergarten mentioned) Investor: ➖ (good liquidity if priced right, but “penthouse/65 m²/3+living” is a story you’ll need to defend) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (balcony, top floors, lots of nearby cafes/parks; easy transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional layout + amenities, but the price-per-m² looks optimistic for a small 65 m² unit unless the building/finishes/location truly punch above Durlești norms.

Negotiation strategy

Start by reframing it as a compact 65 m² unit (not a “penthouse premium” unless there’s a real terrace/view/ceiling-height story). Ask for: building year + developer, insulation/roof warranty (top-floor risk), monthly maintenance, heating docs, parking rights, and any encumbrances. Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, furnished, top-floor(ish) new-build-style apartment with 2 bathrooms and strong day-to-day convenience. You are NOT buying: guaranteed “penthouse” upside—without true penthouse features, you’re paying for marketing. If roof/elevator/build quality checks out, it’s a comfortable live-in pick; if not, walk fast.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” shell finish: good bones, but you still need a full interior budget + timeline.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec list (underground parking, elevator to parking, monolithic frame) but you’re paying for “premium” marketing while taking on finishing risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have real comps here). Ask for either (1) a straight discount, or (2) developer-paid upgrades (better windows/door, electrical package, bathroom rough-in, AC prep) and clarify what “white variant” actually includes. If they push “installments from developer,” negotiate the total price down anyway (rate terms can hide margin).

Bottom line

You are buying: a newer-build 3-room (~87 m²) in a managed residence with elevator + underground parking + quieter surroundings. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment, nor a high-street, café-heavy walkable neighborhood — you’ll be driving/transiting for most daily life. Watch-outs: finish quality, noise insulation claims vs reality, and total all-in cost (apartment + parking + fit-out).

€550,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Constantin Stere
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances, autonomous heating) — but verify build quality/maintenance typical for “new construction” in the area.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great central micro-location and a big, usable layout, but the ask looks “trophy pricing”; you need proof (comps, building quality, HOA, parking reality) before paying full.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor hard on price-per-m²: at 127 m² and €550k you’re around €4.3k/m² (very premium for Chișinău unless the building is genuinely top-shelf). Comps are unknown from the data provided, so treat this as a hypothesis, not a fact. Target price range: €460k–€515k (roughly €3.6k–€4.1k/m²) unless the building/finishes/parking/security are clearly best-in-class. Ask for: building year/developer, monthly maintenance, heating costs, full legal pack, noise exposure, elevator condition, parking rules (is it truly assigned or just “open”).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, renovated, central family-sized apartment in a highly walkable pocket with parks and daily amenities. You are NOT buying: guaranteed “prime global-city” liquidity — resale depends heavily on building reputation, view/noise, and whether the renovation is actually high quality. You’re also not buying: certainty on parking value unless it’s deeded/allocated in writing.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — livable only after a real renovation budget (it’s explicitly “needs repair”).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 separate rooms + 2 bathrooms + storage + playground nearby) Investor: ➖ (renovation + 8th floor risk; rentable later, but capex upfront) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (more “practical base” than “vibes”; depends on light/noise/orientation)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/building type is a solid family staple, but “needs repair” + 8/9 floor means you should be paid back in price for renovation risk and building-condition unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €80,000–€85,000 (best-effort; comps not provided). Anchor on renovation scope + top-floor adjacency + elevator/building maintenance uncertainty. Concrete lever to push: ask for (1) recent elevator service record and (2) building/roof maintenance status for the upper floors; if either is weak/unclear, that’s a measurable justification for a bigger haircut.

Bottom line

You are buying: a classic, functional 3-room family layout (MS series) with real space (≈71 m²) and two balconies. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment — you’re signing up to manage renovation cost, timelines, and finish quality. Two ways this deal goes wrong: renovation reveals hidden electrical/plumbing issues (cost blowout), and the 8/9 floor reality (elevator reliability / roof & top-floor water risk / wind-noise) becomes a daily annoyance.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“euro”), fully furnished/equipped, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout + turnkey condition, but the 1st-floor risk bundle (noise/privacy/security/resale) should be paid for in the price.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps not provided): €175k–€190k. Anchor on verifiable uncertainties: (1) 1st-floor privacy/noise (ask to measure/confirm street-facing vs courtyard-facing rooms and window quality), (2) building/association basics for a 15-floor new build (elevator maintenance costs, sinking fund, utility bills). Ask for: proof of autonomous heating docs/service history, and confirm the “subterranean parking” terms (is it included, reserved, or optional/add-on).

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern, turnkey Ciocana apartment with a family-friendly plan (separate rooms, 2 baths) and efficient heating. You are NOT buying: a quiet/ultra-private unit by default—1st floor can mean street noise, weaker light, and harder resale unless orientation is favorable. Two ways this deal can go wrong: 1) it’s street-facing and you discover constant traffic noise / reduced privacy after moving; 2) “fully furnished” looks good in photos but hides mediocre build/finishes and higher-than-expected monthly building/elevator costs. One clean negotiation lever: tie your offer to a condition check—orientation + noise exposure and the exact parking arrangement in writing; if either is worse/unclear, price steps down.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Este în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Street (from listing fields): Ialoveni.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

#49
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Sculeni, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Sculeni, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€125,000 · 3 rooms · Sculeni · str. Calea Iesilor
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated, furnished, appliances), but quality depends on what “Euroreparație” actually means in this block.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “liveable package” (64 m², 2 baths, elevator, parking claim), but you must price in building/neighbor noise + renovation durability.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112,000–€118,000 (comps unknown; this is a risk-adjusted range, not a comp-based valuation). Negotiation levers: (1) request proof/details for the “open parking” (is it assigned/authorized or just informal yard parking?) and price the uncertainty; (2) verify elevator condition + upcoming building works/fees for a 9-floor block—use any ambiguity or upcoming capex to justify a discount. How this deal can go wrong (concrete): - Renovation is cosmetic: laminate/finishes hide uneven floors, damp spots, or weak sound insulation; you discover issues only after moving in. - “Park + quiet” doesn’t equal “silent”: 6th floor in a 9-floor block can still get road noise (Calea Ieșilor) and elevator/neighbor noise; if windows/orientation aren’t favorable, comfort drops.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical, ready-to-live 3-room in a green, serviceable area with good everyday infrastructure. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet/parking certainty or verified renovation/building-quality—those need to be proven. If the docs/building status check out and noise/orientation works for you, it’s a solid “life apartment,” not a trophy asset.

€118,900 · 4 rooms · Ciocana · str. Petru Zadnipru
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated + furnished + appliances), but quality can range from “fresh” to “cosmetic cover-up” — assume a verification visit is needed.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout (78 m², 4 rooms, 2 baths, 2 balconies) in a convenient micro-area, but price needs to reflect panel-block risks, renovation quality, and unclear orientation/noise.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €108k–€115k (comps unknown from provided data; treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push hardest on (1) documented renovation scope/receipts + wiring/plumbing proof, (2) building condition (elevator, roof, stairwell), (3) any damp/mold signals from balconies/bathrooms. Ask for a time-boxed offer contingent on a short technical inspection. Concrete lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: “Euro-renovation” quality — request receipts, contractor contacts, and let an electrician/plumber do a 30–45 min spot-check; if issues are found, reduce price or require fixes.

Bottom line

You are buying space and function: 4-room flexibility, two bathrooms, balconies, and everyday convenience (groceries/parks/transit nearby). You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet or guaranteed build quality — the deal can turn on traffic noise, elevator/building maintenance, and whether the renovation is durable or just cosmetic. Two ways this can go wrong: renovation hides moisture problems (especially around balconies/2 baths) or the building’s common areas/elevator/roof are in poor shape leading to ongoing costs and resale friction.

Negotiation strategy

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (fresh renovation, furnished, reportedly never lived-in) — but verify workmanship + papers.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great micro-location + turnkey renovation, but price needs to reflect unknowns (building type, parking, soundproofing, legal/tech condition).

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on the unknowns: building structure/maintenance ("cotileț" style can mean charming but uneven upkeep), insulation/noise (ultra-central), utilities/heating setup, and renovation documentation/warranties. Ask for: cadastral extract, layout plan, proof of 75 m², who did the renovation + receipts, and confirmation all alterations are registered. Target price range (best-effort guess, comps not provided): €165,000–€178,000. If everything checks out (quiet, solid building, clean docs, quality materials), stretching toward €180k+ is defensible; if any red flags (humidity, weak HOA, unregistered changes), push below €165k or walk.

Bottom line

You are buying a renovated, ultra-central, flexible layout that can work for a family or premium city rental. You are NOT buying certainty on building quality, parking, or long-term quiet without doing due diligence. At this price, the building and paperwork must be spotless — not just the photos.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout and micro-area convenience, but first-floor risk + agency-style fluff + missing hard building facts should translate into a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €80k–€85k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Use concrete uncertainty as leverage: (1) first-floor noise/privacy + security, (2) moisture/smell risk for floor 1, (3) verify actual balcony situation (description says no balcony) and whether “80 mp” is measured properly. Ask to see utility bills and check heating/hot water setup and recent renovations. If any dampness, old plumbing, or weak windows/doors show up — push to the low end.

Bottom line

You are buying: an 80 m², 3-room, practical family apartment in Botanica with strong everyday infrastructure (parks, schools, groceries, transit). You are NOT buying: a premium, quiet, high-privacy unit — first floor can mean street noise, foot traffic, and moisture surprises. Two ways this deal can go wrong: hidden dampness/ventilation issues typical of ground/first floors; and “cosmetic repair” masking tired plumbing/electrics (especially with 2 bathrooms). Negotiation lever: condition proof — make your offer contingent on a clean damp/odour check + confirmation of heating/plumbing status (and any building-level repairs planned).

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 12 food/drink places, 2 parks/playgrounds, and 7 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated, furnished, equipped, with elevator access (8/9) — but verify what “high-quality finishes” actually means in photos and on-site.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout and micro-location; price needs to reflect “secondary” building realities and potential Muncești traffic/noise.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €99,000–€105,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Levers: (1) confirm building condition and elevator reliability for 8th floor; (2) confirm window orientation and noise level from Muncești; (3) confirm the renovation age/quality + what furniture/appliances are actually included in the sale. Ask to see utility bills (winter heating cost) and any building/HOA maintenance plan—use uncertainty here to justify a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying space efficiency: 73 m² with 2 bathrooms and a balcony in a convenient, amenity-dense pocket. You are NOT buying a quiet, premium-building experience; the road exposure + secondary stock can surprise you. If it’s calm inside with windows closed and the building is well-managed, it’s a solid live-in deal at the right number.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated, modern windows) — but ground-floor specifics can change the whole story.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (86 m², 3 rooms + living, schools/parks/transit nearby) Investor: ➖ (likely rentable, but price/ground-floor resale liquidity are the swing factors) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (good access, but floor 1 can mean less “nice-to-live-in” feel depending on light/noise)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid layout and Buiucani convenience, but the 1st-floor risk + unclear building/comfort details mean you should not pay “top of market”.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165,000–€175,000 (best-effort; comps not provided). Your leverage is the floor-1 discount and any uncertainty you can verify: street noise, window orientation, humidity/odor, and the building’s ground-floor security/entry quality. Ask for (a) utility bills + heating type confirmation, (b) proof of building commissioning/association fees, (c) daylight/video walk-through at mid-day.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, family-friendly 86 m² unit in a convenient Buiucani pocket with parks and daily needs close. You are NOT buying: a quiet, airy “upper-floor” feel — if it faces traffic or has poor light, it will always trade at a discount. If it’s calm, bright, and dry for a ground floor, it becomes a practical win; if not, it’s a permanent compromise.

Negotiation strategy
