Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” shell + autonomous heating + double glazing is a strong base, but assume you still need a real finish budget (floors/doors/kitchen/baths) unless proven otherwise.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the location signals are excellent and the layout spec is compelling, but “white variant” + unknown build quality/HOA/utility costs means the price needs to reflect your finishing and risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €140k–€148k (best-effort guess; I don’t have true comps here). Use (1) white-variant finishing costs, (2) elevator/management quality questions, (3) floor 11 exposure (wind/heat/pressure) as levers. Ask for: developer/house docs, utility bills estimate, sound insulation proof/feedback, and a detailed list of what “white variant” includes (wiring, heating boiler/radiators, plaster level, bathroom rough-ins).

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, high-floor, amenity-dense Botanica base with autonomous heating and a practical 2-bath setup. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort (unless the finish is already done to a real standard) or guaranteed quiet/low monthly costs without verifying building management and construction quality. You’re paying for convenience + future finish flexibility — make sure the numbers still work after a realistic fit-out budget.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — delivered as “white variant” (shell for your own finishes) and handover is only in Q3 2026.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive spec (brick, sound insulation, underfloor heating, autonomous heating, underground parking) but you’re buying a promise (2026) in a low-amenity micro-pocket; price needs to compensate for time + execution risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €98k–€103k (best-effort; comps unknown). Levers: (1) Ask for a written finish/spec annex (exact underfloor heating coverage, boiler brand, window/panorama spec, soundproofing standard) and use any ambiguity to negotiate. (2) Tie a discount to a clear payment schedule or to a parking inclusion/upgrade. (3) Push for penalty/compensation language for delivery slippage (even small) — if they won’t, that’s a pricing lever.

Bottom line

You are buying a quiet, club-house style new-build near parks with modern systems and a family-friendly courtyard. You are NOT buying central Chișinău walkability — you’ll drive for most daily life. The deal goes wrong if delivery slips materially or if “white variant” hides costly fit-out/utility surprises that blow your true all-in price.

Ad · Maps · Street View

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new renovation + furnished + A/C, with a practical 3BR + living layout.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in a workable micro-area, but price needs to be justified by building quality, exact layout, and finishing level.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165k–€172k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps here, so treat this as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Ask for: (1) full plan + exact net area vs total, (2) building commissioning/acceptance status + any ongoing works, (3) monthly maintenance costs, (4) photos/video of common areas + elevator, (5) what exactly stays (appliances, furniture brands), (6) parking situation. Leverage: “developer listing” + furnished “new repair” often has markup; push on any vague specs (area discrepancy 85 vs 88 m², quality of finishes, noise/exposure).

Bottom line

You are buying a newer-build, mid-floor, family-friendly apartment with low friction move-in (furnishings + A/C + 2 baths). You are NOT buying a proven ‘prime central’ location; it’s more practical-city living than aspirational address. If the building is solid and the layout is efficient (no wasted corridors), it’s a good deal at the right number — not automatically at ask.

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~35.4/km², grocery density ~21.2/km², transit-stop density ~17.7/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

  • Price: €176,500
  • Rooms: 3
  • Area: Chișinău — Ciocana
#61
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€275,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · strada Liviu Deleanu, 14/16, Chișinău (Buiucani)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, sold furnished/equipped) — but “penthouse + big terrace” always warrants building-envelope checks.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good space, but top-floor + terrace adds maintenance/risk) Investor: ❌ (premium finish + terrace is niche; resale/rent pool narrower) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (privacy, light, outdoor living, top-floor calm)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong lifestyle product, but the price needs to reflect top-floor/terrace risk and the true build quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €245,000–€265,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so treat as a starting anchor, not a valuation). Levers to push on (tie to verifiable uncertainties): - Terrace legality + waterproofing: ask for documents/photos of membrane works, drainage plan, and any past leak repairs; if unclear, price cut or escrow. - Building systems: confirm elevator presence/condition (7/7 matters), heating type and monthly bills; unknowns = negotiate. - Sun/wind/noise reality: require a daytime + late-evening terrace visit; if wind/noise is worse than expected, adjust price. Two concrete ways this deal goes wrong: - Terrace/roof waterproofing fails → recurring leaks, interior damage, HOA disputes, expensive repairs. - Terrace is partially non-compliant / treated as “bonus space” in docs → financing/appraisal issues and weaker resale.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, privacy-first lifestyle apartment with a genuinely usable outdoor space. You are NOT buying: a “set-and-forget” low-maintenance unit — roof/terrace and top-floor systems become your problem faster. If the terrace and building envelope check out, it’s a high-quality daily-living upgrade; if they don’t, it’s a money pit in nice furniture.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Locuința are o suprafață totală de 97,4 m², la care se adaugă o terasă generoasă de 30 m² – un adevărat punct forte al acestui apartament.”.

Additional stated detail: “Terasa dispune de acces direct atât din bucătărie, cât și din una dintre camere, fiind perfectă pentru momente de relaxare în aer liber sau amenajarea unei zone lounge.”. Also noted: “grup sanitar modern”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 5 grocery shops, 2 food/drink places, 118 parks/playgrounds, and 4 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

#62
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€199,900 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · Chișinău, sectorul Ciocana, bd. Mircea cel Bătrân
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: modern renovation + furnished + appliances, plus autonomous heating and underfloor heating reads like “zero hassle” day‑1 living.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready, new block, 100 m², 2 baths” package, but boulevard frontage + unknown building quality/association costs mean price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €180k–€192k (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted anchor). Push on (1) boulevard noise/dust, (2) parking being “open” not secured/owned, (3) agency “0% commission” still means they’re paid—use it to demand a cleaner price, (4) verify floor/heat bills/maintenance fees and any mortgage/encumbrance documents. If they won’t move on price, ask for inclusions (all furniture + appliances), minor fixes, and a faster notarization timeline.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, practical 3-room in a newer building—family-friendly layout with 2 bathrooms and comfort upgrades (autonomous + underfloor heat). You are NOT buying: a quiet, charming central neighbourhood experience; boulevard-adjacent living can trade convenience for noise. Treat it as a “high-comfort Ciocana base” and pay like one—no premium unless the building + orientation prove exceptional.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s a new-build “club house” concept but this unit is unfinished, so timeline/cost hinge on your fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong low-rise/low-unit-count product with good specs, but unfinished delivery risk + price needs to reflect fit-out and any developer/paperwork unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €132k–€140k (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range vs. €147.3k list). Ask for: (1) clear delivery timeline + penalties, (2) what exactly is included in “unfinished” (utilities to door? screed? plaster? boiler?), (3) parking price/availability in writing, (4) warranty scope, (5) soundproofing spec proof, and (6) elevator/maintenance fee estimate.

Bottom line

You are buying a small, privacy-forward building (5 floors / 46 units) with autonomous heating + underfloor heating potential and family-friendly courtyard. You are NOT buying turnkey certainty — you’ll manage finishing costs, contractor coordination, and developer execution risk. If you negotiate properly, this can be a very comfortable long-term “live there” apartment rather than a pure yield play.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong package for Botanica (new premium tower, amenities, good daily walkability), but it’s developer pricing for an unfinished unit, so your edge is in terms, timing, and upgrades.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145,000–€155,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have fresh comps for Etalon / Decebal–Dacia). Push on: (1) finishing credit or turnkey option, (2) parking inclusion/discount (subterranean spot is often where margin hides), (3) payment schedule milestones, (4) snagging/warranty terms, (5) floorplan orientation and window-to-wall ratio (panoramic glass can mean heat/glare/noise exposure).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern high-rise lifestyle product—concierge, underground parking, smart-home wiring, and a very convenient Botanica node. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in, quiet/low-traffic serenity (crossroads location), or a “cheap sqm” deal. If the layout/orientation is good and the developer’s execution is real, this can be a flagship Botanica apartment—just don’t overpay for the word “premium.”

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Apartament cu planimetrie practică, potrivit pentru familie. Amplasare excelentă pe una dintre principalele artere ale sectorului Botanica, cu acces rapid la transport public, centre comerciale, școli și alte facilită…”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 3 food/drink places, 66 parks/playgrounds, and 8 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Dacia is described as the main artery of the Botanica sector, running from Piața Ovidiu to șoseaua Muncești and towards the airport area; the article also mentions the City Gates („Porțile Orașului”) mark the entry into the city from the airport direction. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Dacia din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Botanica.

Street (from listing fields): Dacia.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~10.6/km², grocery density ~28.3/km², transit-stop density ~28.3/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Dacia is described as the main artery of the Botanica sector, running from Piața Ovidiu to șoseaua Muncești and towards the airport area; the article also mentions the City Gates („Porțile Orașului”) mark the entry into the city from the airport direction. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

Sources: Bulevardul Dacia din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

€212,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Chișinău, Botanica, ул. Гренобле
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: furnished, quality appliances, autonomous heating, AC, elevator; layout is already “done” (no obvious renovation signal).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong turnkey duplex (130 m², 2 bathrooms, 2 balconies, parking) but Botanica micro-location here reads more “green/residential” than “walk-to-restaurants,” and duplex resale liquidity is usually lower.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195k–€205k (best-effort; I don’t have verified comps for this building/street). Anchor on (1) duplex complexity/maintenance, (2) any elevator/roof/top-floor noise risk at 10/11, (3) confirm parking is actually usable/assigned (not just “open”), and (4) validate monthly costs for autonomous heating + building admin. If documents/condition are flawless and parking is truly secured, expect to land closer to €205k.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern, two-level “house-in-the-sky” that’s ready to live in immediately. You are NOT buying: a café-and-nightlife walkable hotspot; expect more car/transit dependence for evening buzz. Your leverage: premium price + niche layout + top-floor risk = discount, even when the interior is great.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (marketed as repaired + furnished; autonomous heating is a real comfort upgrade).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good “new-build, high-floor, turnkey” package, but the ask looks like it’s priced for perfect condition/location and you should verify build quality + true layout.

Negotiation strategy

Start by stress-testing the listing’s “3 rooms” claim (it reads like 3 bedrooms + separate kitchen), then use objective hooks: floor 11/13 (wind/elevator dependence), monthly maintenance/association fees, and any missing essentials (parking, storage, sound insulation). Target price range (best-effort): €255,000–€270,000. If comparable sales in Sculeni/new builds confirm the premium and the renovation is genuinely high-end, expect them to defend closer to €275k–€285k.

Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, newer-build, turnkey family apartment on a main corridor with strong daily convenience. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet (arterial road), nor guaranteed “luxury” unless the finishes, windows, and building engineering check out in person. If the building is solid and the interior is actually premium, it’s a comfortable long-term hold — but don’t pay top-of-market without comps and a technical sniff test.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + location signals, but price needs to compensate for 1st-floor trade-offs and the real finishing budget.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €152,000–€160,000 (comps unknown, so this is risk-adjusted, not comp-driven). Push hardest on: (1) 1st-floor discount, (2) “white variant” finishing cost, (3) any building/HOA or developer handover gaps. Negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: ask for the exact “white variant” specification + handover акт/acceptance docs (what’s included: screed, plaster level, electrical points, bathroom rough-ins). If it’s thinner than implied, quantify your finishing delta and price it in.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large-format 3-bedroom in a new-build block with autonomous heating and solid everyday walkability. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort—budget/time for finishes + furnishing is part of the deal. The 1st-floor reality can be great for access, but you must be picky about noise, privacy, and humidity proofing.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €180,000–€190,000 (best-effort guess; true comps unknown). You are NOT buying: a high-street, amenity-dense, transit-rich location (OSM shows very limited food/drink and no mapped transit within ~300m). Final verdict

Negotiation strategy

Ask for hard proof: exact floor/total floors, building year/type, monthly maintenance/HOA, heating system specs and bills, parking legal status (owned vs assigned), terrace waterproofing warranty, and any permits for interior changes. Use “liquidity + verification” as leverage: penthouses take longer to move, and buyers will discount uncertainty. Target price range: €440k–€475k (best-effort). If comps for this building/streets confirm true top-of-market trades, you may have to live closer to ask; comps are not provided here, so assume negotiation room.

Bottom line

You are buying a high-spec, high-comfort central penthouse with real “daily luxury” (3 baths, storage, terrace) and a walkable, amenity-rich micro-area. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet or easy resale—central zones plus niche pricing can amplify noise sensitivity and time-on-market risk. If the building/parking/terrace paperwork is clean, this is a lifestyle win; if not, the premium finishes won’t save the deal.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#73
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Telecentru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Telecentru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€109,808 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · Chișinău, Telecentru, str. Ialoveni, 100
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build “white finish” means you still budget time + cash for full interior finishing.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong size/specs on paper, but top-floor risks + developer-marketing vibes + unclear immediate daily-walk amenities.

Negotiation strategy

Start by treating this as: price for shell + finishing + parking decisions. Ask what exactly is included in the “white finish” (electrics, screed, heating loops, boiler, doors, insulation spec) and whether the listed price includes VAT/fees and the underground spot. Target price range: €99k–€106k (best-effort; comps not provided). If they won’t move on price, push for value: underground parking included/discounted, finishing credit, or a staged payment plan with penalties for delays.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3BR+living in a low-rise new-build with 2 bathrooms, terrace/balconies, and a “quiet residential complex” proposition. You are NOT buying: proven neighborhood buzz or a highly walkable daily-life setup right at your door. Top-floor living can be great — but only if the terrace/waterproofing, roof insulation, and elevator reliability are genuinely solid.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new-build, “euro” renovation, furnished, autonomous heating; 7/9 with elevator).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive layout + new-build brick + turnkey furnishing, but the ask looks “finished-product priced”; verify noise/traffic and paperwork before paying top-of-market.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165,000–€172,000 (comps unknown; this is a best-effort anchor for a 2010s/2020s new-build turnkey unit). Levers: (1) ask for proof of total area + balcony/terrace inclusion and recalc price-per-m², (2) document pack (ownership extract, cadastral plan, acceptance/commissioning, utilities contracts, HOA fees), (3) confirm actual furniture/appliances included (model list) and warranty/renovation age.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, turnkey, family-friendly new-build apartment with a “closed complex” feel. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet: street exposure + 7th-floor wind/heat + elevator/core-wall noise can make or break comfort. If the docs/area check out and it’s quiet at peak traffic hours, it’s a solid Telecentru pick — but don’t overpay for the staging.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — it’s a “white box” new-build unit: livable only after you budget/time the full interior fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid new-build basics (autonomous heat, elevator, connected utilities) but “variantă albă” shifts cost/risk to you; price needs to compensate for finishing.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €97k–€103k (best-effort; comps unknown). Anchor on (1) finish-out cost and timelines, (2) any uncertainty around actual usable area vs total, and (3) building handover / quality gaps typical of new blocks. Ask for a written list of what’s included in “connected communications” (meters, radiators/boiler status, electrical panel readiness).

Bottom line

You are buying a modern shell in a taller new block: good structure, autonomy, and long-term maintainability. You are NOT buying move-in-ready comfort — you’re signing up for renovation management, dust, and cost overruns. If the building’s execution is mediocre (noise insulation, elevator reliability, heating setup), the ‘new-build premium’ evaporates fast.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new renovation + furnished/with appliances + autonomous heating suggests low immediate capex (but verify roof/engineering since it’s top floor).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (84 m², 3 bedrooms over 2 levels, 2 bathrooms, playground + nearby services) Investor: ➖ (rentable, but duplex/top-floor narrows tenant pool; resale liquidity depends on building quality) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (quiet area, cafes/parks nearby; duplex feel is “house-like”)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + turnkey condition, but 10/10 top floor + “new build/fond locativ” claims need diligence; price should reflect risk and building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €160k–€170k (best-effort; comps unknown). Anchor on top-floor risk (heat/roof/wind noise), elevator reliability, and ask for proof of: building commissioning/association docs, autonomous heating legality/service history, and any warranty on renovation/roof. If documents + building management/security are truly strong, concede upward.

Bottom line

You are buying: a turnkey, duplex-style 3-room with two bathrooms and decent nearby amenities. You are NOT buying: guaranteed elevator/roof serenity—top-floor comfort and maintenance discipline will make or break this. If the building is well-run (security, cameras, common areas) this can be a very livable “urban townhouse” substitute; if not, the 10th-floor downside hits harder.

Negotiation strategy

You are NOT buying: turnkey living—expect a full interior project and the usual new-build "surprises" (snags, systems, common areas, fees). Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong practical location and a usable layout, but the ask looks premium for 71 sqm on a low floor unless the building is top-tier and impeccably maintained.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €168 000–€178 000. Start by anchoring on €/sqm logic: 185k for 71 sqm implies ~2,600 €/sqm (comps unknown). Push for a target range of 165,000–175,000 € unless they can prove standout advantages (excellent building management, quiet courtyard orientation, secure parking, low utility costs, no legal/technical issues). Demand: maintenance fees/association details, elevator/service history, heating type and winter bills, and confirmation of ownership/docs; use any uncertainty to justify price reduction.

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, family-practical apartment in Buiucani with solid transit access and lots of nearby green space. You are NOT buying: a guaranteed “prime, lively” pedestrian micro-location or an automatic investment slam-dunk at this price. Low floor means: easier daily life (stairs backup) but typically less privacy and more street/entrance noise risk—orientation matters.

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Acces direct la transport public, licee, grădiniță, școală. magazine. parc”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 2 grocery shops, 3 food/drink places, 88 parks/playgrounds, and 12 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

Street (from listing fields): ул. Василе Лупу.

#79
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€235,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · ул. Василе Лупу, 28, Chișinău (Buiucani)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated; basic modern comfort signals).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid layout + floor 2 in a newer block, but the ask price reads “top of market” for the amenity mix shown.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for the full spec pack (year of build, developer, insulation, heating type, monthly maintenance, parking, elevator count, cadastral docs). Then negotiate on price-per-m²: at 235k for 87 m² you’re roughly in the ~2.7k €/m² zone, which is often a premium unless the building/finishes/parking are truly A-tier. Target price range: €205,000–€225,000 (best-effort estimate; I don’t have local closed-sale comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, family-leaning apartment in a green, practical part of Buiucani with good transit reach. You are NOT buying: a hyper-urban, restaurant-heavy micro-location—or a slam-dunk “can’t miss” deal at this sticker price. If the building is genuinely high-quality (construction, heating, soundproofing, parking), it can justify more; if not, the price should come down.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — modern building, “Euro renovation,” sold furnished/equipped, autonomous heating + warm floors.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the apartment spec is strong (97 m², renovated, furnished, high floor, 4 elevators), but you need to confirm build quality, monthly maintenance, and noise/wind reality at floor 14 before paying top money.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235,000–€250,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) high-floor risks (wind/noise/elevator dependency), (2) “open” parking (not guaranteed), (3) verify renovation receipts/brands and warranty status, (4) confirm heating costs + actual building energy performance, (5) confirm legal status of “private yard” and what you truly get access to.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, efficient-feeling family apartment that’s already finished and equipped. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/low-wind comfort at height, or a locked-in parking solution, or a proven ‘best deal’ without comp validation.

Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” shell means you’re buying a project, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great headline price for 77 m² and 3 rooms, but no elevator + top-floor duplex + renovation budget should be priced in hard.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on: (1) no elevator to floors 6–7, (2) finish-out cost/risk (white variant), (3) potential heat/roof/top-floor issues and resale friction. Target price range: €43,000–€47,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps from this exact micro-area/building type). Ask for: confirmation of roof condition/any past leaks, insulation, heating setup, legal/technical docs for the 2-level configuration, and a realistic all-in estimate for finishing.

Bottom line

You are buying: a cheap-per-m² duplex layout with 2 bathrooms and decent everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: elevator convenience, turnkey livability, or an easy future resale to every buyer segment. If you can handle stairs and you have a controlled renovation plan, it can be a value play; otherwise it’s a fatigue generator.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€259,900 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · str. Bogdan Voievod, 1, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” shell finish means you’re buying layout + building, not a finished home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong new-build fundamentals and micro-area convenience, but top-floor/terrace risk + developer/HOA realities should earn you a meaningful discount or paid add-ons.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235,000–€250,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Levers to use: - Terrace + top-floor risk: request written waterproofing spec/warranty scope and maintenance responsibility; if unclear, price must drop. - “95 m²” claim: insist on documented net vs gross area (balconies/terrace rules); if measurement differs, reprice €/m². - Parking: confirm what “open” means vs underground availability/cost; if no guaranteed spot, negotiate a discount or a parking inclusion.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern high-floor, new-build 3-bedroom footprint in Râșcani with strong everyday amenity density (food, groceries, parks, transit). You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort on day one — you’re taking finish-cost risk, schedule risk, and top-floor/terrace exposure. Two ways this goes wrong fast: (1) terrace waterproofing or façade sealing issues leading to leaks/mold and disputes over who pays; (2) elevator/roof/HVAC reliability or high HOA costs in a 17/17 building making monthly ownership feel expensive even if the purchase price was “fine.”

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances; expect only inspection-driven fixes).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional family layout and micro-location signals, but 5/5 top-floor risk (heat/roof, stairs/elevator unknown) should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor on top-floor + building/roof condition + elevator uncertainty + “eurorepair” quality variance. Ask for: roof maintenance history, insulation/overheating proof, utility bills (autonomous heating), HOA/security fees, and a daylight/ventilation check in all rooms. Target price range: €125k–€132k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If they can document recent roof work + low bills + solid building management/security, you can accept closer to ask.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, ready-to-live family apartment in Râșcani with park-side orientation and strong everyday convenience. You are NOT buying: a “set-and-forget” unit—top-floor building risk and comfort (summer heat, roof) must be verified. If the building is well-run and the roof is proven solid, this becomes a very practical long-term home.

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional package (72 m², 2 bathrooms, insulation/radiators, elevator) but panel building + high floor + boulevard exposure should buy you a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a price concession based on (1) panel building, (2) cosmetic-only finish, (3) 8/9 floor dependence on elevator, and (4) likely noise/dust from bd. Dacia. Target price range: €85,000–€90,000 (best-effort; without verified comps, treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

You are buying space efficiency: 72 m² with real family ergonomics (2 bathrooms + storage + balconies) in a transit-served pocket. You are NOT buying a quiet, boutique building experience; this reads like pragmatic “good bones” city living. If the view/light is solid and the balconies aren’t compromised, this can be a very livable Botanica base — at the right price.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 6 grocery shops, 18 food/drink places, 3 parks/playgrounds, and 11 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Dacia is described as the main artery of the Botanica sector, running from Piața Ovidiu to șoseaua Muncești and towards the airport area; the article also mentions the City Gates („Porțile Orașului”) mark the entry into the city from the airport direction. Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Dacia din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Vasile Lupu, 28, sectorul Buiucani, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating + AC.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in Buiucani, but you should pressure-test the €/m² and building/maintenance details before paying a premium.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170,000–€178,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so validate against recent sales in Buiucani new-builds). Use the seller’s “credit with 30% down” pitch as leverage (it signals they want volume/velocity). Ask for: proof of 71 m² measured area (gross vs net), exact room count definition ("3 + living" can hide a small bedroom), monthly maintenance/HOA fees, elevator uptime/service contract, courtyard/parking rules (allocated vs first-come), and any outstanding developer/utility issues. If paperwork is clean, trade price for speed: fast notary date + minimal conditions.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, family-friendly, closed-courtyard new-build unit with autonomous heating and a practical layout. You are NOT buying a nightlife/tourist micro-location; it’s more calm-residential than “step outside into a packed restaurant street.” You’re paying for convenience (finished + furnished) — make sure the finish quality and building management actually justify it.

#86
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€289,000 · 3 rooms · Buiucani · str. Liviu Deleanu, 10/18, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (modern design + “ready to live”), but verify build quality + waterproofing on the terrace.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout story (2 beds + open living/kitchen + 2 baths + terrace) but pricing is hard to justify without sqm/floor/building details.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €255k–€275k (best-effort guess; comps unknown from the provided data). Use one lever you can prove: insist on documented terrace spec/waterproofing + any building handover/acceptance papers (developer sale). If they can’t show it cleanly, price comes down or they fix/guarantee in writing. Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) terrace/green-exit becomes a maintenance/moisture problem (leaks, thermal bridging, mold) that’s expensive and messy; (2) “developer” build quality + shared-building systems (noise, ventilation, heating/hot water reliability) disappoint in real life, and resale becomes harder at this ticket.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, liveable 3-room with a premium-feeling lifestyle hook (terrace to greenery) in a calmer Buiucani pocket. You are NOT buying: guaranteed value-at-any-price — without sqm, floor, building type/year, and terrace details, this can be an overpay. If the terrace is legit and the building is quiet/well-run, this is a “pay for comfort” buy; if not, it’s a long negotiation or a walk.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — “Euro renovation” + built-in furniture/appliances + modern systems reads like a turnkey high-floor unit.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/specs are strong, but €398k in Botanica needs the building story (construction quality, management fees, parking, paperwork) to justify it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €350k–€380k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Anchor on: unknown building class/age, 12/16 floor liquidity risk, any noise from Dacia Blvd, and require proof of autonomous heating legality + renovation receipts/warranties. Make the offer conditional on: full ownership docs, no encumbrances, technical passport matching the current layout, and a clean utilities/association statement.

Bottom line

You are buying a large, modern-feeling family apartment with real separation (4 bedrooms) and “premium comfort” features (heated floors, autonomous heating, branded kit). You are NOT buying quiet suburbia — Dacia Blvd can mean traffic/noise; high-floor living also means elevator dependency. At this price, the building’s quality, maintenance, and parking situation matter almost as much as the apartment itself.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — compelling layout and size for the price, but Poșta Veche micro-location looks quiet and the “developer listing” risk means you must buy only after paperwork + build quality checks.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €145k–€152k (best-effort; true comps unknown). Ask for: (1) written finishing spec (floors, doors, bathroom fit-out), (2) warranty terms for roof/terrace and façade, (3) confirmation of elevator servicing + building management fees, (4) bundle/discount for underground parking, (5) snag list + retention holdback until defects fixed.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, top-floor “penthouse-like” new-build with multiple outdoor spaces and a flexible 2-living layout. You are NOT buying: a walk-out-the-door restaurant street or high-tourist buzz; the immediate 300m POI profile reads residential/low-amenity. Treat terrace + top-floor as the main risk surface: waterproofing, heat gain, wind, noise, and elevator reliability.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, autonomous heating, sold furnished/with appliances).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 rooms, 2nd floor, parks/transit/shops density suggests daily convenience) Investor: ➖ (good location liquidity, but yield unknown without sqm + rent comps) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Centru walkability vibe; lots of food/drink nearby)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “easy life” package in Centru, but price is impossible to validate without total sqm and building condition details.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort guess; comps/sqm unknown). Anchor your offer on (1) missing/unclear total area, (2) 5-floor walk-up dynamics (noise/maintenance/stairwell), (3) verify autonomous heating legality/service history, (4) insulation/renovation quality proof (photos, invoices), and (5) building capital repairs fund / HOA situation. If the sqm turns out small for a 3-room, push harder.

Bottom line

You are buying: a renovated, turnkey 3-room in a central, amenity-dense micro-area with winter comfort upside (autonomous heating). You are NOT buying: a fully “risk-free” deal until you confirm sqm, building type/condition, and the heating system’s documentation. If paperwork + sqm check out, this is the kind of Centru apartment that’s easy to live in and relatively easy to resell.

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” in a new-build usually means you’re buying a high-quality shell + systems, but you still need full finishes (floors/doors/kitchen/baths details).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the build spec and developer pitch sound strong, but the low/ground level and “not finished” status are your leverage; verify noise/privacy and the true finishing scope.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €121k–€128k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Ask for either a direct discount or developer-paid upgrades (bathroom rough-ins confirmed, better door package, AC pre-wiring, parking discount). Use: (1) low/ground floor, (2) finishing budget uncertainty, (3) any view/privacy compromises. If they won’t move on price, push for extras and a clear delivery/hand-over checklist.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build unit with strong “spec-sheet” signals (monolithic frame, insulated facade, premium elevator, autonomous gas boiler) in a green-ish, serviceable Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: instant move-in simplicity or guaranteed quiet/privacy — the floor level can be the make-or-break, and finishing costs/timelines are on you unless negotiated. You are also not buying: a touristy/restaurant-dense core; this looks more residential with decent groceries + transit access.

Parking is a plus, but confirm it’s truly usable/assigned vs “open/first-come.”

Without verified comps for this exact ExFactor complex, assume negotiation is possible: target 185,000–198,000€ depending on what’s included (furniture/appliances), building quality, and any hidden costs (maintenance fees, parking, documents). Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, turnkey 3-room in a greener Buiucani pocket with a “new + finished” premium. You are NOT buying a central/foot-traffic location (POI density for food/drink is modest; transit count looks limited). (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

#96
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€97,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · bd. Decebal, 68/2, Botanica, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic renovation + furnished + autonomous heating suggests you can live there immediately, but expect “finish-quality” variability.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional 3-room layout in Botanica with good everyday amenities, but the price only works if heating/docs/building condition check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (best-effort; I don’t have verified local comps for this micro-spot on Decebal). Levers to use: (1) verify autonomous heating paperwork + service history (boiler age, maintenance, acceptance) and price any replacement; (2) confirm the “59 m²” is official cadastral/registered area (not including balcony or marketing rounding) and adjust €/m² accordingly; (3) secondary-block noise/insolation check—if it faces parking/courtyard or has weak daylight, push harder. Two ways this deal can go wrong: (a) “cosmetic repair” hides tired wiring/plumbing or damp around bathrooms (you have 2), turning into a renovation; (b) heating system is near end-of-life or non-compliant, forcing a sudden €1–3k+ spend and admin hassle.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, mid-floor, family-friendly 3-room with strong day-to-day convenience (groceries/transit/parks nearby) and autonomous heating. You are NOT buying: a premium building/product—expect ordinary stairwell/commons, and the finish quality may be “good enough” rather than durable. If the docs + heating check clean and daylight/noise are acceptable, it’s a workable buy—otherwise treat it as a price play.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “cosmetic” condition suggests livable, but the two-level layout + terrace add hidden maintenance/finishing risk.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 separate bedrooms + extra outdoor space) Investor: ➖ (niche duplex product; tenant pool may be narrower than a standard 2–3 bed) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (terrace + grill + low-density building = big quality-of-life upside)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong lifestyle layout (118 sqm, terrace, small building) but pricing hinges on build quality, roof/terrace waterproofing, and exact micro-location noise/parking.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115k–€122k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Push on (1) terrace/roof waterproofing proof + maintenance responsibility, (2) documented total area breakdown (interior vs balconies/terrace), (3) heating/utility costs for a duplex, and (4) any parking arrangement (written).

Bottom line

You are buying: a “house-like” duplex apartment with real outdoor living (terrace/grill) in a low-apartment building. You are NOT buying: a simple, low-risk standard flat — terraces can become leak problems and duplex heating bills can surprise. If the terrace/roof and papers check out, this can be a great long-term live-in; if not, it turns into a slow, expensive fix that kills resale liquidity.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong product (top-floor + terrace + new build) but the price needs to reflect the remaining fit-out and any penthouse/top-floor risks.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €190k–€205k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified local comps here). Anchor on (1) “white variant” finishing cost, (2) top-floor heat/waterproofing risk, (3) parking being extra, and (4) any elevator/terrace maintenance realities. Ask for: written spec of what “white variant” includes, proof of building commissioning/acceptance, and terrace waterproofing warranty details.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, top-floor 7/7 penthouse-style layout with terrace and autonomous heating in a green Buiucani pocket. You are NOT buying: a turnkey interior (budget meaningful extra) or guaranteed quiet/comfort unless the terrace insulation + elevator + roof details check out. If parking matters, price it in now because it’s not included.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#102
Apartament cu 4 approx. mere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 4 camere, Buiucani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€200,000 · 4 rooms · Buiucani · пр-т Алба Юлия, 81/5, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances), but verify build quality and any duplex/attic quirks.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great size/value headline, but this deal can turn into a maintenance/comfort problem if roof/insulation/noise or developer/building management is weak.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €175k–€190k (comps unknown; this is a risk-discount for a large duplex + top-floor exposure). Levers: (1) building/roof waterproofing history + any past leaks on 5–6, (2) documented autonomous heating spec + recent bills (big volume can be expensive), (3) confirm the real “useful” area vs total (stairs/terraces), (4) parking status “open” and whether it’s actually assigned/contracted.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern, two-level family-sized apartment in Buiucani with strong walkable amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: a low-hassle, liquidity-friendly unit—this is a specialized large-format home with higher running-cost and resale sensitivity. Two ways it can go wrong: top-floor water/thermal issues (roof/terrace, heat loss) and comfort problems (stair-heavy layout + noise/management quality in a newer building).

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€105,000 · 3 rooms · Rîșcani (Poșta Veche) · str-la Studenților, 11/1, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated), with the usual “check the workmanship + wiring/plumbing” caveat for older stock.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout for a family and good amenity density nearby, but you’re buying an older series building (and 4/5 stairs) so price discipline matters.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €96 000–€101 000. List price is 105,000€ (~1,500€/m² if 70 m² is accurate). Without verified comps for this exact micro-area/building series, treat this as a “test-the-market” ask. Target price range: 95,000–102,000€ (aim low if staircase/building condition/no elevator, noise, or renovation quality shows weaknesses). Ask for: proof of renovation scope (electrics/plumbing), recent utility bills, roof condition (top floors matter), stairwell/entrance maintenance plan, parking reality ("open" often means first-come), and any moisture history.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 70 m² family apartment with good daily infrastructure density and a usable, classic layout. You are NOT buying: a premium “effortless resale” asset or an elevator building; stairs + older block economics come with it. If the renovation is truly solid and the stairwell/building is well-run, this can be a comfortable, low-drama home — at the right price.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Râșcani, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€169,900 · 3 rooms · Râșcani · bd. Renașterii Naționale 33, sectorul Râșcani, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” / unfinished shell — you’re buying a layout + views, not a finished home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (110 m², 2 bathrooms, high-floor light; parks/schools nearby) Investor: ➖ (large ticket + finishing capex; rentability depends on final finish + parking) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (panoramic glazing + 13/16 view, lots of food/drink within ~300 m)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (new build, size, floor heating, high floor) but unfinished status + agency listing means you must price in finishing cost and execution risk.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a full cost picture: developer handover spec, utility connections status, elevator/HOA readiness, and what “included” actually means (meters, boiler, floor heating manifold, windows spec). Target price range: €155,000–€165,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range based on unfinished condition + leverage from multiple similar units in the same building). If they won’t move on price, push for value: parking terms, finishing credit, or a clearly priced turnkey partner.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern 3-room canvas in Râșcani with panoramic potential and a very amenity-dense micro-area. You are NOT buying: certainty on final cost/timeline — finishing, coordination, and quality control are on you. If you hate projects, pass; if you want to customize and can manage contractors, this is a serious contender.

Buyer fit

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + new-build finish, but the price needs to be justified by building quality, view/noise, and true walk/transit access.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €225,000–€238,000 (comps unknown from the provided data; this is a best-effort discount band based on risk/unknowns). Bottom line

You are buying a large, modern, practical family apartment in a new residential complex with 2 baths and real separation of spaces. You are NOT buying a proven “walk-to-restaurants/transit” micro-area experience (at least per nearby POI signals). If the building checks out (management fees, soundproofing, elevator uptime), it’s a solid home — just don’t overpay on vibes.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: cosmetic renovation + old “Brezhnevka/cottage-type” stock can hide plumbing/electrics surprises; autonomous heating is a strong plus.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good layout + location signals + autonomous heating, but older building risk + unclear building quality/docs means price needs to be right.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82k–€86k (best-effort). I don’t have true local comps for this exact micro-street/building, so treat this as a risk-adjusted offer band, not a market fact. Levers: (1) “cosmetic repair” often implies near-term spend (bathroom/kitchen, wiring, windows seals), (2) older 5-floor stock usually brings shared-area wear + upcoming roof/stairwell costs, (3) ask to itemize autonomous heating system age/service history.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 3-room, 2nd-floor unit with 2 balconies and autonomous heating in a well-served Telecentru pocket. You are NOT buying: modern building standards (sound insulation, thermal envelope, tidy HOA management) unless it’s proven. If documents + heating + building maintenance check out, it’s a solid “live there” pick—otherwise price must compensate.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În apropiere se află: Bancă, Farmacie, Grădiniță, Piață, Școală, Supermarket!”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Buiucani (Wikipedia, ro)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” shell: you’re buying layout + warmth + floor, then funding the finish.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional brief (75 m², 3/9, 2 baths, 2 balconies, elevator) but “unfinished” + typical-panel building risk means price must reflect renovation + unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108k–€115k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Levers: (1) “White variant” is not move-in ready — quantify your finishing budget and subtract it from ask; (2) confirm building condition (roof, plumbing risers, elevator, facade) and use any uncertainty as a price lever; (3) verify the exact exposure/orientation and noise (Ion Neculce can be louder depending on windows) — if street-facing, negotiate.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical 3-bed family layout in Buiucani with rare usability perks (2 bathrooms + 2 balconies) on a comfortable mid-floor. You are NOT buying a turnkey home — timeline, finish costs, and contractor execution will decide whether this becomes a “clean win” or a money pit. Two ways this goes wrong: renovation budget creep (electrics/plumbing/screed surprises) and building-wide systems issues (old risers/elevator) that you can’t control but will pay for via fees and hassle.

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “turnkey” package in a convenient Ciocana micro-area, but the ask looks premium and needs justification via comps and building quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €215k–€230k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so validate vs recent 999.md deals for similar 3-room, ~100 m², AMIC/new-build, mid-floor on Mircea cel Bătrân). Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, turnkey 3-room with practical extras (office/cabinet, 2 loggias, elevator, autonomous heat). You are NOT buying: quiet/courtyard serenity—boulevard exposure likely means noise/dust and a view premium you should not overpay for. If build quality + management check out, it’s a solid live-in option; if not, treat it as a convenience buy, not a “forever” asset.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (huge area + multiple baths + closed courtyard + playground) Investor: ➖ (very large, higher liquidity risk; tenant pool is narrower) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (terraces + panoramic glazing + top-floor feel)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “rare product” (280 m² penthouse in a newer building) but you’re buying unfinished space and the resale market for ultra-large units is thinner.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for cost credits tied to white-box completion (HVAC/finishings/lighting/terrace waterproofing confirmation) and clarify what’s included with parking. Target price range: €285k–€305k (comps unknown, so treat this as a leverage band, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: volume + privacy + outdoor space in a modern, amenity-supported building. You are NOT buying: turnkey comfort or an easy-to-flip, mass-market apartment. If terraces and light are your non-negotiables, this is the kind of listing you negotiate hard—not one you ignore.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances), but validate finish quality and actual layout execution.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “new build / full package / 117 m²” proposition, but top-floor + price-per-m² risk means you buy the building quality and micro-location, not just the renovation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €235,000–€250,000 (best-effort; comps unknown—if recent New City closing prices support €2,200+/m², you may be closer to ask). Levers: (1) verify what parking you actually get (right to use vs owned spot; underground vs open; extra cost?), (2) confirm top-floor roof/waterproofing and maintenance history, (3) check heating docs (autonomous heating: approvals, service records, monthly costs). If any of these are weak/unclear, push for a meaningful discount or seller fixes.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, “finished” family-sized unit in a modern complex with elevator, closed yard, and good day-to-day amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet (Bogdan Voievod can be traffic-heavy) or guaranteed build/roof quality just because it’s “new build” and “euro-renovation.” Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) top-floor headaches (roof leaks, summer heat load, elevator downtime = daily pain at etaj 10/10), (2) the “parking” turns out to be non-owned / waitlisted / paid separately, hurting resale and your lived experience. One concrete negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: ask for written proof of the exact parking entitlement (contract clause / cadastral info / HOA rules); if it’s not a dedicated owned spot, use that to anchor your reduction.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: “euro” finish, furnished, autonomous heating, and two bathrooms suggest a turnkey setup (verify actual condition on viewing).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — Strong central location + new-build stack + 82 m², but floor 10/13 and “agency premium” pricing mean you should push hard on price and proof (docs, view, noise).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195k–€210k (comps unknown, so this is a risk-adjusted bid range, not a comp-backed valuation). Ask for: exact legal area breakdown, building handover year, monthly maintenance, parking terms (is a space included or just “available?”), heating system papers, and a written inventory list for furniture/appliances. Use floor 10 + potential boulevard noise/wind + any elevator/parking fees as leverage; if they won’t move on price, negotiate parking inclusion, notary/registration costs, and a proper defect checklist.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, central, family-sized apartment with high convenience (elevator, autonomous heating, two baths) and a dense, walkable micro-area. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet, guaranteed view permanence, or a “cheap” deal—this should be treated as a premium lifestyle purchase unless the price drops. If the building/management is solid and the interior is truly high quality, it’s a good Centru play; if not, the premium evaporates fast.

Move-in readiness

Depends — reads like a new-build “smart home” unit, but listing doesn’t confirm interior finish level or handover status.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (4 rooms + 2 bathrooms + veranda, 9/14 floor typically gives light/views) Investor: ➖ (works only if the complex is truly deliverable + easy to rent; micro-area amenities look thin in the 300m slice) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (promised nearby cafes/park, but immediate 300m POI density looks quiet)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive “family-sized new-build” proposition at a decent headline price, but the big risks are (1) delivery/finish certainty and (2) noise/traffic exposure on Calea Moșilor.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108k–€115k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Use verifiable uncertainties as levers: ask for the exact finish package (white box vs turnkey), written handover timeline, what’s included (smart system, wiring, doors, bathrooms), and the building’s documents/commissioning status. If any of these are vague, price needs to move.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, family-oriented apartment in a developer-led complex with elevator high-floor living and “smart home” marketing. You are NOT buying: a proven, already-vibrant walk-everywhere micro-location (the immediate 300m context looks sparse) or guaranteed quiet (main-road risk). Main ways this deal can go wrong: handover delays / unexpected paid “extras” for the finish; traffic noise/dust or an awkward orientation that kills comfort despite the nice floor.

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Aproape de parc, grădiniță, școală și cafenele”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 2 grocery shops, 1 food/drink places, 0 parks/playgrounds, and 2 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Rîșcani is described as being in the northern part of Chișinău (with historical context for the former village/area).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#114
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€420,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · strada București 26, Centru, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: “white variant” shell means you’ll likely need full finishes + kitchen + lighting to move in.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — looks like a genuinely premium micro-building in central Chișinău, but the “developer shell” finish + headline luxury positioning means you should push hard on total cost-to-move-in and price-per-m².

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €380k–€405k (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Make the seller quantify what’s included vs. not included (finishing scope, terrace waterproofing spec, HVAC/boiler brand, underfloor zones, parking terms). Use (1) white-variant finishing cost, (2) terrace risk/maintenance, and (3) “free parking” being non-deeded/less bankable as leverage. If they won’t move on price, ask for upgrades/finish credit or guaranteed parking allocation in writing.

Bottom line

You are buying: a low-density, security/concierge, high-ceiling central home with a real terrace (rare) and a calm courtyard. You are NOT buying: turnkey luxury at €420k — you’re still funding and managing the finish-out. Also: the immediate area reads more “central + green pockets + transit” than “restaurant strip,” so expect convenient, not buzzing-nightlife-on-your-doorstep.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: it’s a new-build “white-box” finish (you still need a full interior fit-out), but the core perks (autonomous heating, warm floors in key zones, elevator, intercom) set you up for a clean, modern build.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great layout and complex, but you’re paying for the brand/new-build + you must budget the full finishing; road-facing windows should be priced in.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112,000–€118,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have true local comps here). Push on (1) white-box finishing cost + time, (2) main-road exposure (noise/dust), (3) any paid extras unclear in the ad (parking, balcony/veranda finishing, developer handover specifics). If they won’t move on headline price, negotiate “value”: included parking, upgraded glazing/ventilation solutions, or staged payments with a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, family-sized, new-build 3BR with 2 baths in a branded Telecentru complex with strong on-site infrastructure. You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment or a quiet micro-location — this is likely a “close the windows at rush hour” address unless the glazing is excellent. A good deal if you price the fit-out honestly and you’re fine trading serenity for convenience + newer construction.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white box”/unfinished new-build: budget + time for full interior fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + new-build perks, but your real price is (purchase + renovation) and the ad has typical agency vagueness on building/docs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €135k–€145k (best-effort; true comps unknown). Anchor on (1) unfinished condition/fit-out cost, (2) verification items: autonomy heating equipment spec, elevator/maintenance fees, and actual view/noise exposure to bd. Renașterii Naționale. Concrete lever: request a written finish-scope (what’s included in “variantă albă”: wiring, plaster level, screed, radiators/boiler, meters) — if anything is missing, price drops.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large 3-room in a newer brick building, mid-floor (4/12), with autonomous heating + security/video and a very “liveable” micro-area (shops/parks/transit). You are NOT buying: a turnkey home — finishing cost, workmanship, and building management quality will decide whether this is a win. Two ways this deal goes wrong: (1) boulevard-facing noise/air quality makes those panoramic windows a regret; (2) new-build/association issues (fees, elevator reliability, developer punch-list) inflate monthly cost and hassle.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“euro”), furnished, appliances included; elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is compelling (100 m² duplex, turnkey) but 9th/10th-floor duplexes often hide building/roof/elevator and summer-heat risks; price looks “too clean” for the unknowns.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €96 000–€101 000. Target price range (best-effort guess, no comps provided): 95,000–102,000 EUR. Levers: (1) request proof of roof condition / last roof works + any top-floor leakage history (9–10 duplex = your risk); (2) confirm elevator reliability and building administration fees/arrears; (3) verify legal status of the 2-level layout (is it properly registered as 100 m² / 2 levels, not an informal conversion).

Bottom line

You are buying light, space, and a turnkey duplex feel in a high-amenity Buiucani pocket. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet/low-heat living: top floors + panoramic glazing can mean noise/wind/overheating. If documents for the duplex layout + building condition check out, it’s a very livable family home; if not, walk fast.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances), but quality can range from genuinely solid to “fresh paint hiding problems.”

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good layout signals (70 m², 2/5 floor, 2 bathrooms, autonomous heating) but you need to verify renovation quality + building/roof/basement health before paying a premium.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115,000–€121,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps here, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on (1) proof of autonomous heating condition/service history + monthly winter bills, (2) evidence of moisture/noise issues typical for older 5-floor stock, and (3) what exactly stays (brand/model list for appliances, furniture inventory).

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-leaning 3-room in Buiucani with strong everyday infrastructure and “easy living” features (parking, balcony, 2 baths, heating autonomy). You are NOT buying: guaranteed high-end build quality or a certainty that “euro-renovation” won’t age badly; also not a hyper-urban, restaurant-dense block. Two ways this deal can go wrong: renovation shortcuts (electrics/plumbing/tiling) that surface after first winter; building-level issues (basement damp/roof/pipe risers) that your apartment-renovation can’t fix. Concrete lever to negotiate: request a short written confirmation of heating system specs/age + a recent service invoice (or a contractor inspection) — if they can’t provide it, discount for replacement risk.
