Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec package (brick, insulation, REHAU, OTIS, courtyard/parking) but you’re taking delivery/finish risk and the immediate amenity density looks modest.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a developer-style deal: either price relief or value-add (parking discount, storage included, staged payments, or finishing credit). Target price range: €115,000–€120,000 (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted target vs the €122,840 ask at ~€1,550/m²).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, security-forward new-build 3-room with solid “spec-sheet” comforts (autonomous gas heating, good windows, sound isolation, elevator, courtyard). You are NOT buying: immediate street-level bustle or proven building track record until delivery; you’ll still need budget/time for finishing and snagging.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 3 grocery shops, 0 food/drink places, 0 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong micro-location for Ciocana + good feature set, but developer-sale pricing and unknown unit specifics (views/sun/noise/finish quality) mean you should push terms.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112k–€118k (comps unknown from the provided data; use this as an opening band, then tighten after checking same-complex resales and delivered-quality reviews). Ask for: (1) upgraded windows/door package confirmation, (2) a written spec sheet for sound insulation, (3) parking/storage bundle discount, (4) staged payments or a cash discount vs the 4-year installments, (5) clear delivery/penalty terms.

Bottom line

You are buying a new-build 3-room in a convenience-heavy node (near a major mall + transit + daily services). You are NOT buying instant move-in — the “white box” finish shifts cost and risk onto you. If the unit faces a busy boulevard, you’re also buying traffic noise risk; verify orientation and insulation in writing.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” finish means you’re buying a shell you must complete (kitchen/baths/finishes).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout in a new block, but the area signals “car/transit dependent” and you still have a full finishing budget to swallow.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a discount explicitly tied to (1) finishing costs for a white-variant unit and (2) any building/area downsides (noise from Vadul lui Vodă corridor, parking reality, courtyard quality). Target price range: €102,000–€108,000 (comps not provided; this is a risk-adjusted ask, not a proven market comp). If they won’t move on price, push for seller/agency to cover part of finishing (or include materials/contractor package) and confirm all utilities + autonomous heating documentation.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build 3-room with sane family layout (2 baths) on a low floor (2/9) that you can finish “your way.” You are NOT buying: a turn-key home or a naturally walkable, amenity-dense neighborhood pocket right outside the entrance. If you’re okay managing renovations and you rely on car/transit anyway, this can be a solid value play at the right price.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances + autonomous heating), but you still want a hard check on wiring/plumbing and any “fresh paint” cover-ups.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good size and practical layout for a family, but it’s 5/9 in secondary stock and the listing language feels “agency-polished,” so price should reflect any building/HOA and renovation quality risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €130,000–€138,000 (best-effort guess; no comps provided, so treat as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Push on (1) secondary building condition (entrance, elevators, roof, pipes), (2) proof of autonomous heating legality/servicing, (3) actual room count vs “2 + living” wording inconsistency, (4) furniture/appliances age, and (5) balcony condition/waterproofing. Ask for utility bills (winter) and any renovation receipts.

Bottom line

You are buying space and a functional family setup (95 m², individual partitioning, two balconies) in a well-served daily-life micro-area. You are NOT buying a prime-central, nightlife-on-foot neighbourhood — it’s more practical than charming. If the building is well-run and the renovation is real (not cosmetic), this can be a solid “live in for years” pick at the right price.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated, furnished, equipped, autonomous heating, elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong central location + big size, but the price is “top shelf”; you’re buying the building quality and layout as much as the address.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: exact building year/developer, maintenance fee, winter heating bills, sound insulation, parking rights (assigned vs. first-come), terrace waterproofing docs, and a daylight/video walkthrough (including elevator lobby + stairwell). Target price range: €395k–€420k (best-effort without comps; if the building is truly high-quality new construction and the terrace is substantial, the seller may hold closer to ask).

Bottom line

You are buying a large, renovated, high-floor center apartment with amenities and daily convenience. You are NOT buying guaranteed quiet (center) or guaranteed parking certainty (open parking can be chaotic). If the building/developer checks out and the terrace is proven leak-free, this is a “pay-up-for-comfort” kind of deal.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive “penthouse” angle + autonomous heating + elevator, but delivery risk (2026), finishing budget, and confusing room/bedroom layout are leverage.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82k–€88k (best-effort guess; no comps provided, so treat as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Ask for one of: (1) price cut reflecting full finishing cost, or (2) developer/agent-paid finishing package, or (3) staged payments tied to construction milestones (not just a date). Concrete lever: the listing’s internal inconsistency (3 rooms vs “2 rooms + living” vs “3 bedrooms + living”) — require updated floor plan + cadastral/technical docs and renegotiate if net usable area / room count differs.

Bottom line

You are buying: top-floor new-build space with terrace potential and autonomous heating, in a greenish micro-area. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home, a proven building, or a walk-out-the-door café/restaurant street life (nearby POI mix looks sparse). Two ways this goes wrong: (1) handover slips past 2026 and ties up capital; (2) “penthouse/terrace” ends up compromised (wind, waterproofing, noise from roof equipment) and becomes a maintenance headache.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (new build, furnished, equipped, with fresh “Euro” renovation) — but verify workmanship.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2 separate bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, playground/closed yard) Investor: ➖ (strong “rentable” spec, but price level + 11th floor narrows tenant pool) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (heated floors, autonomous heating, on-site shops, decent nearby parks/transit)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive turnkey 65 m² layout in a modern complex, but you’re paying a premium; make the building/docs and noise/wind exposure prove it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €160,000–€170,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted bid range, not a market-precise valuation). Ask for: (1) written confirmation of total monthly building/maintenance fees + sinking fund, (2) proof/terms for the underground parking (included, optional, or separate), (3) heating system documentation (autonomous unit model, warranties) and underfloor heating zones. Use any uncertainty here as a concrete lever to justify your discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, high-floor, turnkey 3-room (2BR+living) in a closed-yard complex with strong convenience. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/low-wind living, nor guaranteed top-tier build quality unless inspection and paperwork check out. If the parking and building economics are clean, it’s a solid “pay for comfort” purchase; if not, walk or price it like a risk project.

  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 5 grocery shops, 8 food/drink places, 7 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

#126
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Durlești, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Durlești, Chișinău mun.)
€116,900 · 3 rooms · Durlești, Centru · or. Durlești, str-la Tudor Vladimirescu
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: recent renovation, furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good turnkey spec for the money, but you should price in building/club-house reality + noise/parking + heating/docs checks before paying the ask.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105,000–€112,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have verified comps for this exact street/building). Levers: - "Club house" / new-build positioning: request the building commissioning/acceptance paperwork and HOA/service-fee clarity; if anything is vague, push price. - Autonomous heating: ask for the boiler brand/age, last service proof, and winter bills; uncertainty here is a clean bargaining chip. - Floor 3/7 + balcony: verify orientation and street noise (evening + morning) and use any noise/low-light issue to negotiate.

Bottom line

You are buying: a 65 m², modern-finished, furnished 2-bed + living in a newer block with autonomous heat — low friction to move in. You are NOT buying: a guaranteed “prime center” lifestyle; Durlești can feel suburban and street-by-street. Main risk: you overpay for finishings while the building management/parking/noise reality drags quality-of-life and resale.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: “Euro renovation”, furnished, appliances installed, autonomous heating + underfloor heating suggests a turnkey spec.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong package (new build, security, parking potential, turnkey finish), but price is the whole story: without confirmed sqm/condition proof beyond broker text and without comps, you should push for value or extras.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €170k–€178k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If seller won’t move, negotiate “value add”: include a parking spot / storage, confirm high-quality materials with invoices, and require a clean technical/ownership file. Use the “71 m² for 4 rooms” angle: tighter room sizes can be a future resale objection.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, guarded, amenity-rich new-build setup in Buiucani that’s optimized for day-to-day living. You are NOT buying generous room proportions; this is an efficiency plan where layout quality matters more than raw square meters. If the building is truly well-managed (24/7 security, closed yard, functioning elevators), this can be a very comfortable long-term base — but don’t overpay without comp support.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build “white variant” means you’re buying a shell + systems, then paying for the full interior fit-out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout and top-floor package are strong, but you’re taking 2025 completion risk and you’ll spend real money finishing it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €145k–€155k (comps unknown; this is a risk/finishing-cost discount, not a comp-based valuation). Push on: (1) delivery date + penalties in contract, (2) what exactly “white variant” includes, (3) fixed price for parking, (4) elevator/roof/terrace waterproofing warranties, (5) whether the 115.8 sqm is net vs gross and how terrace is counted.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, top-floor new-build with terrace + 2 baths in a planned complex, with good “future comfort” signals (autonomous heating, insulation, underground parking). You are NOT buying: instant walkable café life right outside (low food/drink POIs in 300m) or a finished, turnkey interior — budget time + cash for fit-out and snagging.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white box” finish in a new(er) building means you’re paying for potential, but you still have a full fit-out (kitchen/baths/doors/lighting) ahead.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good building/area story and functional 3-room layout, but you must price in finishing + verify the exact building quality/docs and noise/wind exposure on the 9th floor.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €140k–€148k (best-effort without comps; if the seller has strong demand, you may land closer to ask). Anchor on: (1) “white box” finishing budget, (2) 9th-floor exposure (wind/noise/heat gain), (3) any uncertainty on 2-bathroom plumbing/ventilation execution. Concrete lever: request written confirmation of commissioning/acceptance documents + exact autonomous heating specs/brand + last-12-month building maintenance fee/utilities; if any are missing/unclear, reduce price or demand seller-paid finishing credit.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, security-managed building in Buiucani with a family-friendly layout and strong daily convenience. You are NOT buying: a turnkey home — you’re taking on budget risk and contractor risk. Two ways this deal goes wrong: the “white box” hides uneven walls/screed/insulation issues that blow up finishing costs; or the 9th-floor orientation brings persistent noise/wind/overheating that you can’t fix with furniture.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for (1) exact handover condition (white box vs fully finished), (2) what’s included (kitchen, doors, bathrooms), (3) heating/utility costs + management fee, (4) terrace waterproofing warranty, (5) parking price included or separate. Target price range (best-effort guess, comps unknown): €128k–€138k if parking is included and finishes are decent; if it’s unfinished or parking is extra, push lower or demand developer concessions (fit-out credit / parking discount / extended warranty).

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, new-build, top-floor lifestyle apartment with terrace/loggia and “modern-complex” perks (elevator, video, underground parking). You are NOT buying: a plug-and-play central, restaurant-rich neighborhood vibe—this reads more like parks + basics + transit, not an evening street-life hub.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “new-build + full fit-out” package, but at this price in Ciocana you’re paying for finish/complex; make the numbers and building ops bulletproof.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps unknown): €235,000–€245,000. Levers: (1) require proof/clarity on what exactly stays (furniture list + brands/models), (2) confirm underground parking is included vs optional and price it separately, (3) check monthly building fees + sinking fund + management quality, (4) verify sound/thermal insulation claims with an on-site “quiet test” (neighbors, corridor, elevator shaft), (5) use any cosmetic/maintenance notes from viewing to justify a trim.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, family-friendly, security-heavy new-build in a practical, amenity-dense pocket. You are NOT buying a central, nightlife-first location—this is convenience and building quality over prestige. If parking isn’t included, treat that as a real cost and renegotiate the headline price accordingly.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “În imediata apropiere se regăsesc diverse magazine alimentare și de uz casnic, sucursale bancare, farmacii, restaurante, grădiniță, școală”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 11 food/drink places, 101 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~38.9/km², grocery density ~24.8/km², transit-stop density ~17.7/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

€168,000 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · str. Ialoveni, 136, Chișinău (sector Telecentru)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: finished “euro” renovation + furnished + appliances, so you’re largely paying for convenience.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid spec on paper (newer build, 90 m², 2 baths, 8/12), but the immediate walkable amenity signal is weak and price needs to reflect that.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on uncertainty: no clear on-page proof of transit/amenities right by the building and parking info is inconsistent (open vs “subterranean”). Ask for: exact parking type/title, monthly maintenance, heating type/costs, sound insulation, and a daytime+evening noise check (road + nearby traffic). Target price range (best-effort guess, comps unknown): €155k–€162k if parking isn’t deeded/guaranteed; €160k–€168k only if a real secured spot is included and the interior finish is genuinely top-tier.

Bottom line

You are buying a large, modern-ish, family-friendly apartment with a practical layout and low friction move-in. You are NOT buying a walk-out-the-door urban lifestyle; expect some car dependency and do a commute/noise reality check. If the parking and operating costs don’t check out, this is an easy price haircut, not a deal-breaker.

#133
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Durlești, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Durlești, Chișinău mun.)
€110,500 · 3 rooms · Centru · Durlești, Centru, str. Regina Elisabeta, 2
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — “white variant” means you’re buying a shell + terrace, not a finished home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout/terrace/new-build package, but top-floor + finishing budget + documents/terrace waterproofing risk mean price must be right.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €99k–€106k (best-effort; comps not provided). Anchor on (1) finishing costs for a 109 m² shell, (2) 8/8 top-floor heat + wind exposure, and (3) terrace waterproofing / drainage uncertainty. Ask for written confirmation of: legal status of the 42 m² terrace (included in title vs common area use), warranty scope, and what exactly is included in “white variant” (electrics, plumbing rough-ins, screed, отопление hookups). If any ambiguity: request a price cut or seller-paid finishing items.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor new-build penthouse experience + a real outdoor room (42 m² terrace) and the convenience stack (elevator to underground parking, autonomous heating). You are NOT buying: a turnkey apartment or a “no-surprises” building physics situation — terrace details and top-floor performance are where deals go sideways. If you love terraces, pay less upfront and spend the saved money on waterproofing, shading/pergola, and proper HVAC — that’s how you make this one great.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“euro”), furnished, elevator + modern building services.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location and layout, but at ~€235k for ~85 m² you’re paying for the view + park adjacency; make the building/maintenance/quality prove it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €210,000–€225,000 (comps not provided, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on: agency listing, “euro renovation” quality proof (invoices/photos), building-level heating setup/fees, and any noise/traffic from Decebal. If documentation and condition are flawless + view is truly unobstructed, expect less room.

Bottom line

You are buying: a high-floor, modern, park-edge apartment with “daily wow” views and a very livable split. You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-maintenance bargain—boulevard proximity + premium positioning means you must diligence noise, building management, and long-term value.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on day 1, but “cosmetic repair” usually means you’ll want to refresh paint/fixtures and sanity-check wiring/plumbing.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional spec (70 m², 5/9, 2 balconies, elevator, 2.8 m ceilings), but panel building + unclear micro-area convenience means price needs to reflect risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €90,000–€96,000 (best-effort guess; I don’t have true comps for this exact street/building). Use verifiable uncertainties as levers: (1) panel-block capex: roof, facade insulation, подъезд/common-area condition, elevator maintenance records; (2) “moldovenească series” layout + 2 balconies: check actual room sizes, kitchen size, and whether balconies are legally integrated (often not). Ask for: last utility bills (winter heating cost), association fees, and any recent capital repairs. Any “needs refresh / smells / damp corners / tired подъезд” should translate directly into euros.

Bottom line

You are buying: a straightforward, family-grade secondary-market apartment in Botanica with solid ceiling height, elevator access, and two balconies. You are NOT buying: a buzzy walk-out-the-door neighborhood with lots of cafes/shops or proven transit convenience. Two ways this goes wrong: hidden building-wide maintenance costs in a panel block; or the “70 m² / 3 rooms” feels smaller in practice due to corridor-heavy layout.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (fresh “turnkey” renovation + appliances); verify build quality and what exactly was replaced.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout + amenity-dense micro-area, but you’re paying for a renovation story; make the price contingent on documentation + actual renovation scope.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €108,000–€114,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a starting bracket, not a valuation). Ask for: (1) proof of renovation scope (wiring/plumbing/risers, not just finishes), (2) confirmation of legal re-planning if the kitchen+living area was reconfigured, and (3) winter heating bills / comfort evidence. Negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: if electrical panel/wiring and plumbing risers were NOT fully replaced (or no receipts/photos), price drops to reflect future risk.

Bottom line

You are buying a practical 3-room, 72 m², 3/5 walk-up in Botanica with a modernized interior and a solid everyday-needs radius. You are NOT buying certainty about hidden systems unless the seller can document the renovation beyond cosmetics. If the courtyard/noise story is real and paperwork is clean, this is a very livable “plug-and-play” option — just don’t overpay for paint and marketing.

Family: ✔ (3 rooms, 78 m², park-adjacent, floor 3) Investor: ➖ (premium view helps, but finishing costs + resale liquidity depend on final execution) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (terrace + direct Dendrarium outlook is the headline)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — you’re paying a “Dendrarium view premium”; make sure the building/documents/finishing economics justify it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €175k–€190k (comps unknown; range assumes new-build premium but discounts for “white variant” + developer/hand-over uncertainty). Levers you can actually use: (1) final measured area & terrace/usable area definition (pay per final cadastre, not ad text), (2) terrace waterproofing/warranty + facade/insulation spec in writing, (3) parking/storage availability and pricing (often where the real cost hides), (4) timeline for acceptance/commissioning and who pays connection/registration fees.

Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build 3-room layout with high ceilings (3.10 m) and a rare park-facing terrace/view. You are NOT buying: a finished, immediately livable apartment (budget and time for fit-out are mandatory). If the view is truly protected and the terrace/build quality is strong, this can age well; if not, you’ll feel the premium every month.

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Terasa cu priveliște direct la Parc și în curte.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 0 grocery shops, 2 food/drink places, 26 parks/playgrounds, and 8 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Buiucani is described as being in the north-west part of Chișinău. The article frames it as an area between Calea Ieșilor, Valea Morilor and the Balcani road, and notes it includes nearby localities such as Durlești/Vatra/Trușeni/Ghidighici/Condrița.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

#138
Apartament cu 4 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 4 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€125,000 · 4 rooms · Botanica · str. Băcioii Noi, Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper ("Euro renovation", furnished, appliances) — but verify build quality and heating costs.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2nd floor, 3 bedrooms + living, schools/transport claimed nearby) Investor: ➖ (good rentability if docs + building management are clean; depends on micro-location liquidity) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (quiet/residential; not a restaurant-heavy, walk-out-the-door vibe)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid “new build + 4 rooms + 65 m² + autonomous heat” package, but the price needs to reflect a quieter/less-amenity-dense micro-area and the usual new-build/document risks.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €112,000–€120,000 (best-effort; I don’t have direct comps for this exact building/micro-street). Levers: - Ask for proof of 65 m² total vs usable, and exact room sizing (4 rooms at 65 m² can feel tight) → price adjustment if layout is compromised. - Confirm autonomous heating type, last winter bills, and whether any building-level issues exist (elevator/HOA/maintenance fees) → use any uncertainty to negotiate. - If it’s truly “agency” inventory, push for a clean, fast-close discount in exchange for deposit + firm timeline.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern-family layout in a newer 9-floor building with a convenient 2nd floor and autonomous heat. You are NOT buying: a high-amenity, café-heavy central lifestyle pocket (OSM nearby shows very low food/grocery density). Two ways this goes wrong: (1) the “new generation” block has weak sound insulation/neighbor noise; (2) paperwork/commissioning status or HOA realities (fees, defects) are messier than the ad implies.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — attractive functional layout (82 m², 2 baths, veranda) and new-build perks, but new-complex inventory and pricing leverage should exist.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €98,000–€103,000 (comps unknown; using leverage from developer inventory + multi-floor availability). Push for either a direct discount or value-adds (parking/storage, finished walls/doors/sanitary, appliance package, or notary/registration fees covered). Bottom line

You are buying: a new-build, family-friendly 3-room layout with 2 baths and modern infrastructure around it. You are NOT buying: scarcity or a unique unit—this reads like a standardized developer product where terms matter as much as the sticker price. Check hard before committing: final finishing scope, elevator/parking reality, soundproofing, and whether the veranda is legally counted/usable year-round.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (quality renovation + autonomous heating + underfloor heat), but confirm build quality + noise/wind exposure at floor 9.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great size/spec in a new-build stack, but Botanica + high floor + “agency polish” pricing means you should buy only if the building, management, and parking are truly strong.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring at 335k–345k and try to land 350k–360k (comps not provided, so this is a leverage-based range, not a comp-based valuation). Ask to itemize what’s included (parking spots, furniture/appliances, storage) and use any gaps (no included parking, weak HOA, poor soundproofing, low-quality finishes) to push price down.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern-feeling family apartment with comfort features (autonomous heating + underfloor heat) and a high-floor view. You are NOT buying: a restaurant-heavy “step outside and stroll” micro-neighborhood; you’ll likely be commuting to the fun. At €370k, the building’s execution (elevators, insulation, management, parking reality) matters more than the staging.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not verified): €94 000–€99 000. Target price range (best-effort, no comps provided): 95,000–100,000 EUR. Push on: cosmetic-only renovation (implied future spend), any elevator/entrance wear, and proof of low monthly costs. If documents are clean and the building is well-run, you can accept closer to ask; if not, walk.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical 3-room in Botanica/Dacia with strong everyday infrastructure and transit density. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet, premium building quality, or a renovation that eliminates near-term capex. Check the building systems (elevator/roof/basement) and the apartment’s “hidden” items (windows, wiring, plumbing) before you emotionally commit.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€138,320 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Chișinău, Botanica, Str. Dimineții, 22
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new build with “white-box” finish + delivery scheduled for end of year means you’re buying a timeline and a renovation budget, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong apartment spec on paper (brick, insulation, high floor, parking), but the two big variables (handover + total finishing cost) can swing the real value a lot.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €127k–€134k (best-effort; true comps not provided). If the developer/agency won’t move on €/m², push for value: parking included/discounted, written handover date + penalties, or finish package credit. Negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: make price conditional on (a) documented commissioning/acceptance milestone and (b) exact “white-box” scope list (what’s included: wiring, boiler/heating hookups, screed, doors/windows specs). Ambiguity here is where budgets explode.

Bottom line

You are buying: a high-floor, modern 3-room shell in a new complex with a likely solid envelope (brick + mineral wool) and a clean layout story (2 baths, 2 balconies). You are NOT buying: guaranteed move-in timing or a fixed all-in cost — the finish-out and handover date are the deal. Two ways this goes wrong: (1) handover slips and your payment schedule becomes dead money; (2) “white-box” turns into a €20k–€40k+ finishing bill (and you discover missing items like heating kit, wiring completeness, sound insulation, or elevator/commons not fully finished).

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 3 grocery shops, 0 food/drink places, 0 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new renovation + sold furnished/equipped, in a recently delivered building.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “no-work-needed” package, but you must sanity-check price vs local comps and the building’s execution quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort guess, comps not provided): €175k–€190k. Anchor lower if (a) noisy orientation, (b) weak building management/fees, (c) floor heating/autonomous heating docs are unclear, or (d) finishes are cosmetic over shortcuts. Concrete lever: request building docs + confirmation of heating system specs/maintenance history and use any gaps (or missing warranties) to justify a €10k–€20k reduction.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, turnkey 75 m² plan in a new-build, with good “daily-life” infrastructure and lots of nearby green space. You are NOT buying: a proven, time-tested building (new-build risks) or a central/tourist premium location. Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) panoramic glazing + orientation = overheating/noise/heat-loss, turning utilities and comfort into a constant annoyance; (2) new-build common areas/management underperform (poor maintenance, unexpected fees, construction defects surfacing after move-in).

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — “white variant” (unfinished) means you’re buying a clean shell with autonomy (good), but you’ll still spend real time/money to finish.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and new-build basics, but it’s unfinished and the “Centru” labeling within Stăuceni is marketing; price should reflect fit-out and resale friction.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €105k–€115k (best-effort guess; I don’t have direct comps). Anchor on unfinished condition (“white variant”), unknown exact finish scope, and confirmables (building quality, elevator performance for 9/10 floor, noise/parking). If seller resists, push for seller-paid finishing items or a documented materials/works budget credit.

Bottom line

You are buying a new-build 3-room family layout with autonomous heating, 2 bathrooms, outdoor space, and lots of nearby day-to-day amenities. You are NOT buying turnkey, and you’re not buying the liquidity/premium of a true Chișinău central address. If the building is solid, this is a good “space per euro” play — but only at the right number after you price the finishing.

#146
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Periferie, Dumbrava, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Periferie, Dumbrava, Chișinău mun.)
€145,500 · 3 rooms · Dumbrava · Dumbrava, Codrilor, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated, furnished, equipped, autonomous heating) — but treat it like a small “house-share” building, not a classic apartment block.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good spec (115 m², renovation, autonomy) but the format (unit inside a private 3-level house) + peripheral location should buy you a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €132k–€140k (best-effort guess; no comps provided). Anchor lower if any documentation/partition/utility-meter questions aren’t perfectly clean. Negotiation levers you can verify: - Clarify the legal form: is it a registered apartment unit or a share of a house with usage rights? This directly impacts bank financing and resale. - Confirm autonomous heating is truly independent for your unit (own boiler + meters) and check monthly winter bills. - Parking “open” + yard access: confirm it’s deeded/allocated, not just “we park here”.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large renovated 3-room home with semi-private vibes (separate entrance) in a very green, low-tourism pocket. You are NOT buying: city-center walk-to-cafes convenience or a fully standardized, easy-to-finance commodity apartment. Two ways this goes wrong: (1) documentation/ownership structure limits mortgage options and hurts resale, (2) shared-building realities (noise, maintenance, roof/façade costs) without clear HOA-style governance.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on finishes (designer renovation); make sure the “good investment” claim is backed by invoices, photos of rough-in, and a snag list.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “done” interior in a newer building, but at this price you must verify build quality, monthly fees, and resale liquidity in Ciocana.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €230k–€245k (best-effort; I don’t have reliable comps here). Anchor on: (1) developer/management reputation + any known issues, (2) elevator/roof/water pressure performance at high floors, (3) parking/legal status, (4) proof of renovation scope (electrical/plumbing), (5) any mismatch between “3 rooms + living” wording and legal plan.

Bottom line

You are buying: a spacious, newly-built, high-floor 3-room home with a premium renovation and decent everyday convenience nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed top-tier central lifestyle or automatic investment yield at this ticket price—this needs due diligence and price discipline. You’re paying for finishes; make sure the building fundamentals (maintenance, elevators, noise, heat/water) deserve the premium.

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 11 food/drink places, 101 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 bedrooms + living, 2 bathrooms, close to schools/parks, playground) Investor: ➖ (works if rental demand is strong on this street; price needs to be tight) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (Centru address, elevator, parking, easy transit)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/features are strong, but you should push price because local “walk-out-to-bars” signal looks modest and we don’t have verified comps.

Negotiation strategy

Start with a clean, rational offer anchored on missing/uncertain bits (view/noise, building management, real net area vs. 82 m², parking deeded vs. assigned, quality of the “euro” renovation, utility costs for autonomous heat). Target price range: €200k–€210k (best-effort guess; comps not provided, so validate against 2–3 nearby closed/active sales before moving up).

Bottom line

You are buying a high-floor, modern, turnkey family-sized unit with 2 bathrooms and (claimed) underground parking in Centru. You are NOT buying a hyper-buzzy café street (nearby F&B density looks low), and you shouldn’t pay a “prime micro-location” premium unless the exact spot proves it. If the building is well-run and the parking is truly yours on title, it’s a solid long-hold city base.

#150
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€185,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · Chișinău, Centru, str. Toma Ciorbă
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Likely move-in ready on paper (“ready to move” + appliances), but ground-floor + historic house means you must verify moisture/insulation/heating before believing it.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (good centrality; but 1/1 house + ground-floor privacy/noise trade-offs) Investor: ✔ (strong central micro-area; can work as office/salon if legal/HOA allows) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high ceilings + walkable core; courtyard could be a calm oasis)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — great location + 3.40m ceilings are rare, but ground-floor in a historic building is where hidden problems (humidity, paperwork, heating) live.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for: (1) proof of legal status/registration and any restrictions for commercial use, (2) recent renovation invoices/photos, (3) heating system details + winter bills, (4) moisture readings on exterior walls, (5) courtyard access/ownership rules. Target price range: €160,000–€175,000 (best-effort guess; comps not provided). If docs are clean and it’s truly dry/warm in winter, you can justify closer to ask; if any damp/structural/admin quirks appear, push below €165k or walk.

Bottom line

You are buying: central Chișinău walkability with “old-city” proportions (80 sqm + very high ceilings) and potential dual-use (home or work). You are NOT buying: a low-maintenance modern block experience—ground-floor historic stock can mean ongoing capex, comfort quirks, and resale buyer selectivity. If it’s dry, quiet, and legally clean, it’s a character asset; if not, it’s a money pit in a nice postcode.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “https://ecoimobil.md/ecoimobil_imobiliare/str-toma-ciorba/”.

Additional stated detail: “De vânzare apartament la sol – Centru, Chișinău.”. Also noted: “Se oferă spre vânzare un apartament spațios de 80 m.p., situat la sol, într-o casă istorică din inima orașului Chișinău, pe str. Toma Ciorbă.”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €185 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Etaj, Fond locativ, Grup sanitar, Living, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Locație excelentă: zonă centrală, aproape de toate facilitățile (transport public, magazine, școli, instituții culturale)”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 7 grocery shops, 37 food/drink places, 20 parks/playgrounds, and 6 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Centru (Chișinău) (Wikipedia, ro)

Sector (from listing fields): Centru.

Street (from listing fields): Chișinău.

Sectorul Centru is described as covering the central part of Chișinău (and the town of Codru), and it concentrates many monuments/museums and key institutions. The page also lists central streets/boulevards (e.g., 31 August 1989, București, Armenească).

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, autonomous heating + underfloor heating + A/C, gated courtyard.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong spec and size in a newer building, but price is ambitious for Ciocana and floor 1 carries real trade-offs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €295k–€315k (comps unknown, so treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push on: (1) floor 1 discount vs higher floors, (2) limited immediate transit/foot-traffic signal, (3) verify what “garaj” means (deeded garage vs rented/assigned) and whether it’s included. Ask for: proof of building commissioning/acceptance, HOA/maintenance costs, heating system docs, underfloor heating zones, noise/waterproofing on ground floor, and a daylight/privacy check at different times.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, turnkey, newer-build family apartment with comfort tech (autonomous + underfloor heating) and a more controlled, gated setup. You are NOT buying: a café-and-transit-first location or an “always liquid” resale profile—ground floor + Ciocana means you need the price to do some of the work.

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (furnished + appliances; “ready to move in” positioning) — but verify renovation quality and any “agency lipstick” in person.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the layout/features are compelling, but at 9/10 floor and in a secondary-market building, you need a pricing win and strong building/association checks.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for proof on “no encumbrances,” building maintenance/HOA payments, elevator condition, roof/technical floor issues, and winter heating costs; use any elevator wear, facade/entry deterioration, or noisy exposure (boulevard) to justify a discount. Target price range (best-effort): €160k–€170k (comps not provided; treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation).

Bottom line

You are buying: a family-forward 4-room with real privacy (isolated bedrooms) + 2 bathrooms + elevator building + decent everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet (boulevard risk) or a “prime central” location; plus the 9th floor is a love-it/hate-it factor. If the building is well-run and the interior is genuinely quality, this can be a very solid long-term live-in pick — just don’t overpay.

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (new-build penthouse with smart-home + autonomous heating), but verify finish quality + waterproofing/terrace details.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout and “penthouse/top-floor” appeal, but micro-area looks light on daily walkable life (few food/parks within 300m) and new-build execution risk is real.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €118k–€124k (best-effort guess; I don’t have comps for this exact complex, so treat this as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Push hard on: documented total area (99.8 vs 99), terrace/veranda counted how, warranty/hand-over timeline, elevator spec/maintenance fees, noise/roof heat in summer, and any developer “extras” that are still unbuilt.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, new-build 3-room “statement” apartment with modern systems and a bigger-than-average plan. You are NOT buying: a proven, mature neighborhood micro-core right outside your door (expect more reliance on short drives/transit). If the terrace/roof and building management check out, this can feel premium daily; if they don’t, it becomes an expensive headache fast.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated, furnished, appliances), but the building-level unknowns could still bite.

Buyer fit

Family: ➖ (3-room layout in 50 m² is tight; good if you value schools/parks nearby) Investor: ✔ (easy rentability vibe: transit + groceries + services close) Lifestyle buyer: ➖ (convenient area, but 4/5 walk-up + courtyard parking trade-offs)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “ready-to-live” package in a practical Botanica micro-area, but price looks ambitious for 50 m² in older secondary stock unless the building is unusually solid and quiet.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €90,000–€96,000 (best-effort guess; I do not have verified local comps for this exact street/building). Levers that can be verified fast: (1) confirm building type/condition + any roof/façade/stairwell issues (top-floor-ish risk nearby) and use any visible common-area wear as a price lever; (2) verify the “bilateral orientation” + noise (Independenței traffic) by doing an evening visit with windows open/closed; if it’s loud, that’s money.

Bottom line

You are buying: a compact 3-room (2 bedrooms + living) that’s already finished and positioned for everyday convenience (parks, groceries, transit). You are NOT buying: spaciousness or “new-build comfort” — expect older-building realities, walk-up stairs, and parking competition. Two ways this deal goes wrong: the renovation hides electrical/plumbing shortcuts (cheap ‘eurorepair’ pattern) and you end up redoing systems; the street-facing side is noisier than expected and resale/rent demand suffers unless you discount.

Negotiation strategy

#156
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€149,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · str. Vasile Alecsandri, 17, Centru, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (good condition + autonomous heating; expect only cosmetic tweaks unless inspection surprises).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (central, 92 m², autonomous heating, elevator), but the pricing needs to reflect building/entry condition, noise, and true 3-room layout quality.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for docs and building basics first (maintenance status, any major repairs planned, heating system service history, elevator upkeep, neighbors/noise). Use “secondary stock + mid-floor” as leverage. Target price range: €138k–€145k (comps not provided, so this is a structure-based guess; if finishes are premium and building is clean/well-run, expect closer to ask).

Bottom line

You are buying central convenience + a generous 92 m² footprint with efficient independent heating. You are NOT buying a quiet suburban bubble — verify street noise, parking reality, and building common-area quality. If the layout truly lives like a modern 3-room (2 beds + living), it’s a keeper; if it’s a compromised “2+living” marketed as 3, push harder on price.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (renovated, furnished, appliances, autonomous heat; typical “pack your bags” unit).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional layout and building package, but you should push on price and verify building/floor data consistency before paying top-of-market.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €185,000–€199,000 (best-effort estimate; I don’t have verified comps for this exact Braus complex and finish level). Use: (1) data inconsistency (7/19 mentioned vs “17 floors” elsewhere), (2) ask for proof of autonomous heating costs/maintenance, (3) elevator/HOA fees, (4) furniture/appliance inventory & warranties, (5) any mortgage-eligibility constraints (Prima Casă) to frame a “clean buyer, fast close” discount.

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern, family-friendly 3-room with real usability (wardrobe, 2 baths, 2 balconies) in a practical, well-served pocket of Ciocana. You are NOT buying: a bargain or a historic/quiet boutique block—this is a big-complex, main-boulevard lifestyle where noise/traffic and HOA rules can matter. Check: actual total floors + exact floor, view/noise exposure to bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, and building management reputation.

€139,900 · 3 rooms · Telecentru · Chișinău, Telecentru, Str. Grenoble
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work — it’s a shell/“white/gray” finish, so budget time + cash before you can live in it.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong fundamentals (new brick, top floor, volume) but the price only works if the building/roof/elevator and your finishing budget check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125k–€133k (best-effort; comps not provided, and finishing cost uncertainty is material). Push on: (1) “unfinished” status (you’re funding the fit-out), (2) top-floor/roof risk, (3) agency listing premium. Make the offer conditional on written confirmation of autonomous heating spec + final acceptance docs for the building.

Bottom line

You are buying: top-floor “penthouse” volume (127 m² + 3m ceilings) in a newer brick building, with the chance to design it right. You are NOT buying: move-in-ready comfort, predictable final cost, or guaranteed quiet/thermal performance until you verify windows/roof and do the finish properly. Two ways this can go wrong: roof/terrace waterproofing issues (top floor) and surprisingly high all-in renovation cost (materials + labor + design changes + HVAC). One concrete negotiation lever: verify orientation + glazing spec + summer overheating risk (top floor panoramic windows) — if unclear, price it as a risk and discount accordingly.

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong “daily-life convenience” location and a practical 60 m² layout, but you should price in a walk-up + secondary stock risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €84k–€90k (best-effort; comps not provided). Push on: (1) walk-up (4th floor of 5), (2) “cosmetic” finish ambiguity, (3) document/association/building condition unknowns. Make the offer conditional on verifying the exact floor plan area + utilities/heating type + any recent roof/stairwell repairs.

Bottom line

You are buying: a functional 3-room, ~60 m² apartment in a very practical Botanica micro-area with strong nearby amenities (groceries, transit, parks). You are NOT buying: a modern-renovated, elevator-served building or a “no-surprises” property—condition and building upkeep can bite. If noise/orientation is bad (road-facing) or the stairwell/roof is tired, this becomes a value trap at asking price.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (euro-renovation + furnished + appliances), but verify workmanship and building systems given the high floor.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good micro-location signals and functional spec, but price needs to reflect panel-block risk, high-floor realities, and unknown true condition.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €118k–€128k (comps unknown; this range is a risk-adjusted starting point). Push on: (1) high floor (13/15) + elevator reliability, (2) renovation age/quality proof (receipts, photos pre/post), (3) any moisture/ventilation issues, (4) parking being “open” not guaranteed, (5) balcony listed as “No” (some buyers discount that hard). Ask for: latest utility bills, association fees, and any planned building works.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, functional Râșcani family apartment with strong everyday amenities density and transit access. You are NOT buying: a low-risk building asset without due diligence—high-floor + secondary stock means you must audit elevators, plumbing, and renovation quality. You’re paying for “ready now” convenience; make sure the renovation isn’t just cosmetic.

Move-in ready: recent renovation, sold furnished, kitchen equipped, elevator building.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid turnkey family apartment in a convenient, transit-heavy pocket, but you should pressure-test the price vs. block type/area vibe and verify renovation quality.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (best-effort; true comps not provided). Use: panel-building stock (series 143), floor 5/9, Poșta Veche micro-area calmness vs. limited food/drink density nearby, and “developer/agent” pricing posture. Ask for: proof of renovation scope (wiring/plumbing), exact total vs. living area split, heating bills, and elevator/building maintenance status. If anything is “cosmetic eurorepair only,” anchor closer to the low end.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-friendly, turnkey 3-room with 2 bathrooms and good daily-life infrastructure + transit. You are NOT buying: a prime central vibe or a dense walk-to-dinner neighborhood; this is convenience-first, not lifestyle-first. Pressure-test: renovation quality + building health (elevator, common areas, utilities) — that’s where panel-block deals go from great to annoying.

#162
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€125,900 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Cetatea Albă 3, Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready — “individual design”, furnished, autonomous heat, AC, elevator; low hassle if the finish quality checks out.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional package (86.4 m², floor 2/9, 2 apts per landing, autonomous heating), but the stated bank valuation (~€115k) weakens the €125.9k ask.

Negotiation strategy

Start from the financing reality: if the bank values it ~€115k, many buyers get constrained on leverage. Use that as your anchor and push for seller-side flexibility. Target price range: €112,000–€118,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a disciplined financing-based range, not a market comp claim). Ask for: proof of autonomous heating documentation/servicing, building maintenance fee history, elevator/common-area condition, and a clause covering any unpermitted remodel elements.

Bottom line

You are buying: a larger 3-room + living in a mid-rise block with convenient daily life, low-tourism vibe, and a “second floor” sweet spot. You are NOT buying: a prime central nightlife/walk-everywhere district; this is more practical Botanica than “showpiece downtown”. Watch-outs: verify daylight/noise (street-facing?), finish quality behind the “design” label, and parking reality in the micro-area.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€127,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Chișinău, Botanica, Bulevardul Traian, 1/1
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper (renovated + furnished), but verify renovation quality and building systems typical for panel stock.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong functional package (72 m², 2 baths, balconies, mid-floor, AC, elevator) but “panel/series” + agency-led listing means you should pressure-test price and renovation.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €115,000–€122,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so treat as a negotiation anchor, not a valuation). Ask for: proof of 72 m² in documents, renovation year/photos of wiring/plumbing, heating bill history, elevator/roof/capex plans, and clarify what “separate entrance” actually means (true private entry vs stairwell positioning). Use panel-building concerns + agency boilerplate + any wear in finishes to justify a step-down.

Bottom line

You are buying: a practical, family-friendly 3-room with unusually good “utility” (2 bathrooms, 3 balconies) in a transit-connected Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: a premium new-build experience — panel stock can mean noise/thermal quirks, and “Euro-renovation” varies wildly. If documents + building condition check out, this is a convenience play; if not, it becomes an expensive cosmetics trap.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: new-build, high-spec renovation, furnished + appliances, autonomous gas heating, elevator/intercom.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong product (80 m², 3/10, quality fit-out, only ~3 units per floor) but price feels “top shelf”; you want proof it’s priced like the best, not just marketed like it.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €220k–€235k (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort positioning). Ask for: what exactly is included (furniture/appliances/underground parking), heating costs + maintenance fees, and any remaining developer punch-list/warranty. Use nearby amenity mix as leverage: lots of parks and groceries, but low immediate food/coffee density and only modest transit count within ~300m.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, security-minded, family-friendly building with a “finished and done” interior and easy access to green space. You are NOT buying: a truly central, café-heavy street-life location — expect more “drive/ride to fun” than “step outside to fun.”

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances included — but double-check the heating type (block-level vs “autonomous” wording conflict).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (2 separate bedrooms + living, nearby schools/kindergartens, gated courtyard/playground) Investor: ➖ (good “ready to rent” package, but 15/16 floor + airport-facing view narrows tenant pool) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (high-floor view, on-site services, modern complex with underground parking)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong layout + modern complex + turnkey condition, but price per m² likely ambitious for Botanica unless the finish/building quality and parking situation are genuinely top-tier.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €125,000–€135,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided). Push on (1) high-floor risk (wind/heat, elevator dependence), (2) airport-side noise/air quality sensitivity, (3) clarify what “heating” really is and monthly costs, (4) confirm underground parking terms (included/optional/cost). Offer fast closing contingent on documents + building management fee proof.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern 60 m², 2-bed + living, high-floor “turnkey” unit in a closed-courtyard complex with commerce/services downstairs. You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-floor, easy-stairs backup home — this is elevator-lifestyle and location/exposure matters. If the view is your thing, great; if you’re noise-sensitive, test it at peak hours before falling in love.

Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished, appliances included, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (3 rooms, 2 bathrooms, park access, playground mentioned) Investor: ➖ (good liquidity/location, but premium price—yields depend heavily on exact rent) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (next to Valea Trandafirilor + across from MallDova; easy daily life)

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong location + turnkey spec, but price needs to be justified by building quality, view/noise, and true condition.

Negotiation strategy

Start by verifying the basics that drive value here: exact building class/developer, legal docs, HOA/maintenance, elevator count, insulation quality ("interior insulation" can be a moisture risk), and noise levels facing the mall/arterials. If everything checks out, still negotiate on “turnkey premium”. Target price range: €200,000–€210,000 (comps not provided, so this is a best-effort range based on a negotiation discount from the ask for a 93 m², new-build, renovated unit in a prime Botanica pocket). If any red flags (humidity/ventilation, cheap finishes, noisy exposure), push €185,000–€200,000.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big, modern-feeling 93 m² layout in a very convenient Botanica micro-area with park + mall right there. You are NOT buying: guaranteed quiet/air quality—mall-facing views often mean traffic noise and dust; confirm before paying a premium. This is a “pay for convenience” apartment: worth it if you value park walks + urban access more than absolute calm.

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready: renovated, furnished, appliances in place, autonomous heating.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid turnkey package in a new building, but the price needs to be defended by building quality, noise/orientation, and actual neighborhood walkability beyond “nearby services.”

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €155,000–€165,000. I don’t have true comps here, so treat this as a leverage range, not a valuation. Levers: (1) Ask for proof of autonomous-heating setup and monthly winter bills; if it’s not truly efficient or there are building-wide issues, that’s immediate price leverage. (2) Verify what “security + 24/7 video” actually means (staffed guard vs cameras-only, monthly HOA); unknown recurring costs justify a discount.

Bottom line

You are buying a modern, turnkey 3-room layout (2 beds + living) with balcony and on-site amenities in Poșta Veche. You are NOT buying prime central walk-out life: local POI density looks moderate (very limited nearby food/drink) and the lived experience will depend heavily on the exact micro-location and transport. Two ways this goes wrong: elevator/HOA cost surprises in a 13-floor building; or the unit’s orientation/noise (road/yard) makes “nice renovation” feel cheap day-to-day.

€177,000 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · bd. Mircea cel Bătrân, Chișinău (Ciocana)
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready (high-end finish + fully furnished/equipped; just verify build quality and warranties).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — the package is premium and practical, but you need a reality check on price vs local comps and on the “new build delivered” execution.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €165k–€172k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push on: (1) developer/building track record, (2) actual utility bills for full-floor heating, (3) elevator/entrance upkeep, (4) proof of commissioning/acceptance and any remaining defects list. If everything checks out clean, be ready to pay closer to ask for the turnkey furnishing/appliances.

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern, turnkey 2-bed + living in a 12-floor building with real comfort features (heated floors, AC, quality finishes). You are NOT buying: a quiet, low-density “old-city charm” vibe — it’s a boulevard location with urban noise/traffic trade-offs. Also: the micro-area reads functional (groceries + transit nearby) with lots of green space, but it’s not a restaurant/nightlife hotspot.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Compartimentare: 2 dormitoare, living, bucătărie, antreu, bloc sanitar (dispune de cadă și cabină de duș), balcon.”.

Additional stated detail: “https://ecoimobil.md/ecoimobil_imobiliare/bd-mircea-cel-batran-18/”. Also noted: “De vânzare apartament cu două camere și living, situat în sectorul Ciocana, bd. Mircea cel Bătrân.”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €177 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Balcon/ lojie, Etaj, Euroreparație, Fond locativ, Grup sanitar, Living, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Starea apartamentului, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad text does not name specific nearby amenities, so plan to validate daily-life convenience yourself (walk times, noise, access).

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 8 grocery shops, 8 food/drink places, 86 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Grounded context: OSM density proxy (within ~300m of the pin): food/drink density ~28.3/km², grocery density ~28.3/km², transit-stop density ~17.7/km². (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)
