#169
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€95,000 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · str. Petru Zadnipru, 16, sectorul Ciocana, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Mostly move-in ready: cosmetic renovation + furnished/with appliances, but expect “liveable now, upgrade later” (kitchen/baths/electrics depending on age).

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — good functional layout (75 m², 2 balconies, 2 baths, 8/9 with elevator) but panel building + cosmetic repair means price needs to reflect inevitable refresh and building-condition risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort): €85,000–€92,000 (comps unknown, so treat this as a negotiating anchor, not a valuation). Levers to use: (1) documentable building factors: elevator condition, roof/upper-floor leak history, facade/entrance repairs, and monthly heating/maintenance bills; (2) renovation reality check: “cosmetic” often hides dated wiring/plumbing and tired bathrooms—price the refresh. Two ways this deal can go wrong: a) top-floor-ish reality at 8/9 = noise/heat loss + future roof/elevator special assessments that destroy the “cheap monthly” assumption; b) bilateral orientation helps ventilation, but if one side faces a loud road/yard, your livability (and resale) takes a hit.

Bottom line

You are buying space efficiency: 75 m², practical family layout, two bathrooms, and day-to-day convenience (transit/groceries/parks nearby). You are NOT buying premium building stock—panel series + cosmetic finish = predictable capex later. If the building is well-run and the noise/orientation checks out, it’s a solid “workhorse” home at the right number.

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Needs work: “white variant” finish means you’re buying a new-build shell + systems, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — good new-build package (underground parking, elevator, autonomous heating) but you should price in finishing costs and Durlești liquidity.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for an all-in comparison: base price + any mandatory “extras” + parking terms + finishing timeline. Push for a discount or included items (e.g., partial finishing, kitchen/doors, or parking). Target price range: €88,000–€93,000 (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range based on finish-stage + location + new-build marketing premium).

Bottom line

You are buying: a modern new-build 3-room layout with a developer story (views, underground parking, premium elevator). You are NOT buying: a highly walkable, cafe-dense central neighborhood—this is more “drive/commute and chill.”

#171
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€173,250 · 3 rooms · Botanica · Str. Gradina Botanica, 18, Chișinău, Botanica
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — it’s a new-build delivered “white box”, so you’re buying a finish-out project, not a turnkey home.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the core product (new build, big 3+living, park-facing location) is strong, but price only works if delivery terms, views/noise, and build quality check out.

Negotiation strategy

Target range: €160,000–€170,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range, not a comp-based valuation). Push for concessions if the handover date slips, if parking is priced separately/unclear, or if the “white box” scope is thin. Negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: ask for the exact handover spec (what’s included in “white box”), written delivery timeline/penalties, and the parking deed/rights — any ambiguity here should translate into a lower price or developer-paid upgrades. Two concrete ways this deal can go wrong: 1) Delivery/commissioning slips into next year and you’re stuck with a mortgage clock + rent overlap. 2) The apartment ends up with compromised livability (traffic noise/dust from the main road, poor orientation/light, or developer shortcuts in common areas) that you can’t “renovate away.”

Bottom line

You are buying: a large, modern family apartment in a new complex with a rare green buffer (Botanical Garden) and underground parking. You are NOT buying: a finished, move-in-ready home — you’re committing to capex, timelines, and contractor management. If the view/orientation is good and the handover spec is solid, it’s a very livable long-term hold; if not, it’s an expensive lesson in “new-build marketing vs reality.”

#172
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Centru, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€89,000 · 3 rooms · Centru · bd. Iuri Gagarin, 7, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — secondary-market unit; listing gives location + basics, but no renovation/condition details.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — prime “daily-life convenience” micro-area + park proximity, but the ad is thin on the stuff that makes or breaks value (condition, docs, noise, building health).

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €80,000–€85,000 (best-effort; comps not provided, so this is uncertainty-discount pricing). Negotiation lever tied to a verifiable uncertainty: noise/air quality + orientation on bd. Gagarin — ask for windows type, which side the unit faces, and do a 10-minute balcony/window-open noise check at rush hour; use any weakness (street-facing, old windows) to justify the discount. Two ways this deal can go wrong: (1) street-facing bedrooms + weak windows = permanent noise you can’t “renovate away”; (2) secondary-stock paperwork/technical passport mismatch (room layout, area, balcony, prior re-plans) that blocks mortgage or forces costly legalization.

Bottom line

You are buying: a compact 3-room (56 m²) in Centru with excellent transit density and real nearby park options. You are NOT buying: a guaranteed quiet home or a clearly “turnkey” renovation — the listing doesn’t prove either. If it’s courtyard-facing and documents are clean, it’s a solid city-living play; if it faces the boulevard, price must reflect that reality.

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends: listing text conflicts (“no renovation” vs “ready to move / furnished”), so treat as a cosmetic+systems-risk unit until proven otherwise.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — location and layout are strong for the money, but the “condition” contradiction is your leverage and your main risk.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €85k–€92k (best-effort guess; comps not provided). Push hard on: (1) “Fără reparație” classification, (2) 12/16 elevator reliance, (3) any tired common areas/maintenance. Make the offer conditional on seeing: apartment video walkthrough + utility/heating proof + building management fee details.

Bottom line

You are buying: a 69 m², 3-room, central, high-floor apartment with 2 bathrooms and strong transit access. You are NOT buying: guaranteed move-in-ready condition—expect either renovation spend or at least targeted fixes once you see it. Big ways this goes wrong: (1) high-floor comfort issues (wind/noise/heat loss) + weak windows/insulation, (2) building/elevator maintenance problems that turn “cheap to buy” into “annoying to live.”

Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Move-in ready on paper: renovated (“Euroreparație”), furnished, with appliances, elevator/intercom; verify workmanship + wiring/plumbing.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — solid functional layout (2 baths, 2 balconies, storage) and renovation helps, but price/value hinges on confirmed 74 m² vs the obvious “674 mp” typo, plus building/area noise and true walkability.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €98k–€105k (comps unknown; range is a risk-adjusted ask for 74 m², 8/9 floor, renovated, secondary stock). Push on: (1) renovation age/receipts, (2) elevator + roof/top-floor-adjacent risks (even if you’re not top floor), (3) sound insulation/road noise, (4) building maintenance fund and any upcoming capital repairs. Ask for a daytime + late-evening viewing on a weekday.

Bottom line

You are buying: a “get-things-done” 3-room in Botanica with space efficiency (2 baths, balconies) and a ready-to-live setup. You are NOT buying: a prime walkable café/park micro-neighbourhood right outside the door (based on the limited 300m POI signal). If the building is well-run and the noise is acceptable, it’s a good family home at the right price — not a trophy location deal.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

(On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Еste în imediată apropiere de: parc, grădiniță, școală, piață, cafenele, bancă, farmacii etc.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 2 grocery shops, 0 food/drink places, 0 parks/playgrounds, and 0 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Sector Botanica is described as being in the south-east part of Chișinău (SE of the city) and is an administrative sector of the municipality.

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Sectorul Botanica (Wikipedia, ro)

Move-in readiness

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — great central positioning and usable size, but the “1 of 1 / at ground level” building profile and cosmetic state should be priced with a discount.

Negotiation strategy

Start by anchoring on unknowns: building type/condition, moisture risk (low floor), roof responsibility (if truly top/only level), parking reality, and renovation scope. Target price range (best-effort guess; comps not provided): €105k–€112k. If they won’t move, push for seller-paid fixes/credits (bathroom refresh, paint, electrical check) and proof of heating docs/maintenance.

Bottom line

You are buying: a big-for-price 3-room in Centru with strong daily convenience and easy city access. You are NOT buying: a modern, quiet, amenity-rich building experience—expect quirks, noise, and higher due-diligence on building/roof/ground-floor risks.

Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Botanica, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€306,000 · 3 rooms · Botanica · str. Grenoble, sec. Botanica, Chișinău
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — new-build penthouse in “white finish” with handover stated for 2025 H2, so you’re buying a timeline + a fit-out project.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ (huge layout, 3 bathrooms, storage, terrace; but delivery/finish risk) Investor: ➖ (large ticket, niche penthouse buyer pool; works only if you nail final finish + parking) Lifestyle buyer: ✔ (top-floor, panoramic glazing, terrace, privacy)

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the product is compelling (169 m² penthouse, terrace, premium building spec), but you must price in off-plan/hand-over risk and the full cost of finishing.

Negotiation strategy

Ask for a developer-style package: price concession OR finish/upgrade credits (HVAC, better glazing spec confirmation, terrace waterproofing warranty terms, elevator access to top floor, parking inclusion). Best-effort target: €270k–€295k (comps not provided, so this is a risk-adjusted range rather than a comp-driven “true value”). Push hardest if parking is not included or if handover terms are soft.

Bottom line

You are buying: a top-floor, modern-shell penthouse with real “wow” features (terrace, panoramic windows, high ceilings) in a quieter Botanica pocket. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in certainty or a central, high-liquid resale product — your exit depends on execution and handover. You’re also buying the micro-area vibe: lots of nearby green space signals, decent daily groceries, and workable transit access — more practical than flashy.

FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Depends — sounds structurally “fine” but top-floor + “first line” boulevard exposure means you must verify roof, noise, and windows before calling it plug‑and‑play.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong Botanica positioning and 72 m² is solid, but 10/10 + boulevard frontage is where expensive surprises (roof/heat/noise) hide.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €82,000–€89,000 (comps not provided; this range assumes you’ll price in top-floor/roof risk and boulevard noise unless documentation/renovation quality clearly offsets it). Use a checklist-driven negotiation: 1) roof inspection / last overhaul proof, 2) heating + utility bills, 3) window quality/noise at peak hours, 4) elevator reliability (if any), 5) legal clarity (seller is “developer” + secondary stock).

Bottom line

You are buying convenience in central Botanica: groceries + transit density and a functional family layout with 2 bathrooms. You are NOT buying serenity — “first line” on bd. Traian and a top floor can be amazing or annoying, depending on roof, glazing, and building maintenance. If the building is well-run and the view is real, it can feel premium; if not, it becomes a constant small tax on comfort.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ✔

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong specs (size + parking + finished) but the price is where deals are won/lost.

Negotiation strategy

Anchor off €/m²: at 325k for 175 m² you’re paying ~€1,860/m² (before any “club house” premium). Without verified comps for this micro-area/building, assume negotiation is possible: target €295,000–€315,000 depending on actual building quality, soundproofing, and whether parking is deeded/guaranteed. Use (1) age/quality of building systems, (2) elevator/maintenance fees, (3) sunlight + noise on Ciuflea, and (4) furniture/appliance value realism as leverage.

Bottom line

You are buying space and convenience in Centru with a finished interior and parking. You are NOT buying a “cheap per m²” deal; you’re paying for turnkey + low-friction living. If the building is truly quiet and well-managed, it’s a keeper; if not, the street exposure becomes the tax.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ❌

Final verdict

🟡 NEGOTIATE — the bones look promising (space, top floor, terrace, autonomous heating), but top-floor risk + unknown build/maintenance quality + finishing costs should translate into a meaningful discount.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range: €195,000–€210,000 (best-effort guess; no direct comps provided). Bottom line

You are buying: a very large, top-floor family-sized apartment with terrace potential in Telecentru, with parks nearby and decent transit access. You are NOT buying: turnkey convenience or a restaurant-heavy walkable micro-area (amenity density looks modest within ~300 m). If the roof/terrace and building management are solid, this can feel “penthouse-adjacent”; if not, it can become an expensive ongoing headache.

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — strong size/layout in a functional area, but price-per-m² is the whole story here and you need clarity on build quality, view/noise, and monthly building costs.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps unknown): €215k–€230k. Anchor with: (1) agency listing + duplicated ad text (often signals room to move), (2) verify actual net/livable area vs “total 117 m²” (balconies can inflate), (3) request building docs + HOA/maintenance fees + utility/heating setup; any ambiguity becomes a pricing lever.

Bottom line

You are buying space and a family-friendly layout in a newer block with everyday amenities nearby. You are NOT buying a proven “quiet luxury” asset until you confirm noise (boulevard), construction quality, and ongoing costs. Two ways this deal goes wrong: the boulevard-facing orientation is loud/windy at night, or the “new build” has weak soundproofing / expensive, opaque building maintenance fees. One clean lever: make the offer conditional on written confirmation of heating type, monthly building fees, and a floorplan that clearly separates internal area vs balconies—discount if anything is vague.

Agent assessment — property (ad-anchored)

This note sticks to what the seller actually states in the ad text and what is present as structured fields. Key line from the ad: “Etaj 8 din 10.”.

Additional stated detail: “2 băi.”. Also noted: “Bloc nou, Amplasare reușită aproape de marketuri, farmacii, școli și transport public.”. The ad does not clearly state Exact floor level / storey, Elevator / lift, Outdoor space (balcony/loggia/terrace/garden). Ad warnings: Pin status is unconfirmed; location may be approximate..

What to treat as baseline info: Rooms: 3. (Structured field) Price: €239 000. (Structured field) Panels (999.md): Etaj, Fond locativ, Număr de camere, Număr de etaje, Suprafață totală. (On-page characteristics)

Fit (who this suits)

  • Best fit: buyers who are comfortable relying on a viewing for confirmation and are fine with a short ad.
  • Less ideal if: you need full upfront disclosure (fees, building condition, technical details) before visiting.

Micro-area (local guide)

The ad explicitly points to nearby conveniences: “Bloc nou, Amplasare reușită aproape de marketuri, farmacii, școli și transport public.”. Use this as a starting point, then verify real walk times and the day-to-day feel.

What the web sources say about this area: OSM snapshot within ~300m of the pin shows: 6 grocery shops, 10 food/drink places, 53 parks/playgrounds, and 5 transit stops. (Source: OpenStreetMap / Overpass.) Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

On-the-ground checks

  • Do a 10–15 minute walk loop to confirm errands (groceries, pharmacy, ATM) are realistically convenient.
  • Stand by the building/road for 5 minutes with the window open (if possible) to gauge traffic noise at peak hour.
  • If the building is on a main boulevard, check courtyard/parking access and whether the entrance feels maintained and safe at night.

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

Macro-area (local guide)

Sector (from listing fields): Ciocana.

Street (from listing fields): Mircea cel Bătrân.

Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân is described as being in Chișinău’s Ciocana sector; the article notes it starts at the roundabout with str. Alecu Russo, was built in the 1980s, and has typical urban services along it (shops, post offices, housing blocks, cafes). Sectorul Ciocana is described as being in the eastern part of Chișinău; the article also mentions the sector includes industrial/economic activity and notes green spaces and parks (including a forest-park named „Rîșcani”).

Sources: Bulevardul Mircea cel Bătrân din Chișinău (Wikipedia, ro), Sectorul Ciocana (Wikipedia, ro)

#181
Apartament cu 3 approx. mere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun. (Apartament cu 3 camere, Ciocana, Chișinău, Chișinău mun.)
€153,510 · 3 rooms · Ciocana · strada Ion Dumeniuc, sectorul Ciocana, Chișinău, Moldova
Terrace/OutdoorGarage/ParkingQuiet evenings (proxy)
Ad · Maps · Street View
FACTS BOX (from the ad)

Expert panel (interpretive; not a fact record)

Move-in readiness

Not move-in ready: new-build shell finish (“white variant”) with delivery planned for Dec 2025, so timeline + budget drift risk.

Buyer fit

Family: ✔ Investor: ➖ Lifestyle buyer: ➖

Final verdict

Action: 🟡 NEGOTIATE — interesting price-per-layout potential for 90.3 sqm, but you’re buying a 2025 promise + fit-out costs, so push hard on terms and clarity.

Negotiation strategy

Target price range (best-effort, comps unknown): €140k–€148k, or keep price and extract value (developer-paid VAT/legal/parking/fit-out credits, or a staged payment plan). Concrete lever: ask for a written spec sheet (materials, insulation, windows, heating system) and a fixed handover date with penalties/compensation if delayed. Two ways this goes wrong: (1) Dec 2025 slides and you carry rent + mortgage overlap; (2) “white variant” becomes an open-ended renovation bill (electrics/plumbing finishing, doors, kitchen, flooring) that blows your all-in cost.

Bottom line

You are buying: a larger, modern-shell apartment in Ciocana with strong nearby parks/green signals and decent day-to-day groceries. You are NOT buying: immediate move-in comfort or a fully-known final cost. If the paperwork/specs are tight, this can be a smart “design it your way” play; if not, it’s a budget trap with a calendar.
